useCaseText: HNB's Project Financing business is a 'Total Service offer'. We have a specialised team to understand the commitment your projects require, and can provide you with services beyond the traditional products offered by most banks. We offer services ranging from origination and arrangement, to underwriting and syndication. We also provide financial solutions for corporate restructuring, buy-outs, mergers, and acquisitions.

useCaseText: When you import and export from different shores, it is important to have seamless operational support. Receiving the right trade financing services can help you effectively manage risks associated with foreign trade […]. Import Services: Letters of Credit, Import Documentary Collection, Shipping Guarantees, Trade at your Door step. Import Financing: IFL. Export Services: Letters of Credit (Advising/Confirmation), Export Documentary Collection. Export Financing: Export Bills Purchased / Bills Discounted, Export Negotiation, Packing Credit Loans. Structured Financing: Bank Guarantees (Tender, Performance, Advance Payment, Supply, Sub-Contract, Customs Duty).
description: We offer a comprehensive range of trade & product services so that you can concentrate on the bigger picture. Imports (LCs, IFL, Shipping Guarantees, Doc. Collections), Exports (LC advising/confirmation, doc. collection, Export Bills, Export Negotiation, Packing Credit), and Bank Guarantees (Tender, Performance, Advance Payment, Supply, Sub-Contract, Customs Duty).
currency: Multi-currency (LKR + foreign currencies; trade tariff PDFs list LKR and foreign-currency fees)
tenorText: Packing Credit Loans: pre-shipment working capital — settled out of export proceeds (Trade KFD p.10); IFL: 'Maximum tenor of an Import Finance Loan will not exceed the approved tenor, inclusive of any credit period being enjoyed from the exporter' (Trade KFD p.7)
costText: Commission (calculated percentage of the value, subject to a minimum amount determined by the bank from time to time); SWIFT charge; Document examination fee; Cancellation/amendment charges; IFL: 'Interest as agreed by the bank' + Processing fee (Trade KFD p.7); Packing Credit Loans: 'Interest as agreed with the bank' + Processing fee (Trade KFD p.10); Confirmation of LCs: 'Commission will be decided on case by case basis' (Trade KFD p.8); Government taxes apply
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, Trade KFD)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, Trade KFD)
maxFunding: Packing Credit: 'up to a maximum of 80% of the value of the export order or the letter of credit, against an approved Packing Credit Loan facility' (Trade KFD p.10); Shipping Guarantees: '110% cash as collateral subject to internal credit approvals […] Without collateral or lesser percentage as collateral subject to internal credit approvals' (Trade KFD p.6)
guarantee: Bank Guarantees products (Tender/Bid Bond, Performance Bond, Advance Payment Guarantee, Payment Guarantee, Retention Guarantee, Customs Duty Guarantee) are themselves risk-sharing instruments
eligibilityText: IFL: These loans are granted only to clients having credit facilities with the bank and at rates and tenors agreed & advised by the business units (Trade KFD p.7); Confirmation of LCs: 'Adding confirmation subject to approved credit lines' (Trade KFD p.8); Transferring LCs: 'Availability of this service is subject to discretion of the Bank' (Trade KFD p.9)

productName: Solar Power Generation Loan (ADB-funded — Rooftop Solar PV)
useCaseText: Step 1: Potential solar photovoltaic sub-borrower approaches a solar photovoltaic system supplier who is registered under the SLA and gets a financial proposal (quotation). […] credit facility for the installed rooftop solar system […].
description: Credit facility for installing rooftop solar photovoltaic systems, funded under an ADB line of credit (PFI=Participating Financial Institution; PMU=ADB Project Management Unit). Vendor must be SLSEA-registered; compliance & connection certification by chartered engineer and CEB/LECO required.

costText: Monthly installment for each 100,000: LKR 1,213.28 for 10 years; LKR 2,027.64 for 5 years. [implied effective rate; explicit interest rate not stated in FAQ]
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: Solar FAQ PDF)
minFunding: There is no minimum loan amount.
maxFunding: The maximum loan amount is the equivalent of 50Kw in rupee amount. (According to the pricing of the suppliers)
guarantee: ADB line of credit refinance line (sovereign-channelled DFI funds)
eligibilityText: There are 16 steps to be followed, in addition to the initial screening process to qualify for the credit facility. Vendor must be registered as a limited liability company and registered at the SLSEA. The sub-borrower must apply for individual connection approval from CEB or LECO. PMU will reimburse the eligible cost of the solar project within 03 working days.

useCaseText: Mainly NSB lends to long-term development projects of the country... mostly infrastructure... we do consider any type of project at any stage of the process. i.e. start-up, expansion, on-going projects
description: Project Finance: NSB funds long-term development projects, mostly infrastructure, at any stage (start-up, expansion, on-going). Facilities priced on a variable rate (margin over an index rate re-priced quarterly/bi-annually) with ceiling and floor rates; carry a grace period; capital repaid quarterly or as per project cash-flow. To comply with regulatory requirements most loans are by way of syndications.
tenorText: Capital is re-paid mostly on a quarterly basis or as per scheme that matches with that of cash-flow of the project
costText: Priced on a variable rate. Generally a margin over an index rate re-priced quarterly, bi-annually etc.,... ceiling and a floor rate... Margin is based on the risk profile of the project and promoters... a very nominal processing fee will be charged. (CRIB Corporate processing fee 0.25% per schedule of charges.)
gracePeriod: Generally the loan carries a grace period to allow generate positive cash-flows to repay the capital

maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates, Lakdiriya programme PDF, KFDs)

costText: Loans against Fixed Deposits: Effective Rate + 3% p.a. (≤1 year) / Published Rate + 3% (over 1 year, interest at maturity) (W.E.F. 02.06.2026). Service charges for Loans against Deposits Rs.350; Same day loan against FD Rs.1,000.

productName: Enterprise Sri Lanka & Lakdiriya Concessionary Loan Programme
useCaseText: The Government of Sri Lanka introduced a special credit facility... to financially support every Sri Lankan to become the entrepreneur they dreamt of with Enterprise Sri Lanka concessionary loan schemes... NSB introduced "Lakdiriya", a special loan scheme under the patronage of the Ministry of Public Enterprise and Kandy City Development (MPEKCD) with special interest rates and relaxed credit terms
description: NSB participates in the Government's Enterprise Sri Lanka concessionary loan schemes and operates its own 'Lakdiriya' special-rate programme under the Ministry of Public Enterprise & Kandy City Development (MPEKCD), offering special interest rates and relaxed credit terms across its loan products (Housing, Alankara, Personal, Buddhi, Diriya, Sooryabala/Solar, NSB Reach, Ayojanakawa). Credit counters opened in 22 NSB branches island-wide; 2,983 loans disbursed as at 26 Sep 2018.
tenorText: Per the underlying product (e.g., Diriya up to 5y; Sooryabala up to 7y)
costText: Special (concessionary) interest rates vs Publish rates under the Lakdiriya programme, e.g.: Diriya (personal guarantors ≤Rs.500,000) special 12.00% (Sthree/Ithuru Mithuru 11.00%); Sooryabala (CEB solar) special 12.50%; Ayojanakawa special 11.50%; NSB Reach special 13.50%; Housing special from 12.75%.

eligibilityText: To financially support every Sri Lankan to become an entrepreneur (Enterprise Sri Lanka). Lakdiriya offers special interest rates and relaxed credit terms; concessionary rates for Sthree / Ithuru Mithuru account holders.
eligibleApplicants: Sri Lankan entrepreneurs / borrowers across NSB loan products
businessSizeText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates, Lakdiriya programme PDF, KFDs)
collateralText: Per the underlying loan product (guarantors / property mortgage / cash deposit)
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates, Lakdiriya programme PDF, KFDs)

tenorText: Repayment period Maximum up to 7 years
costText: Competitive interest rate and the lowest monthly rentals; Monthly Rental Rs. 1,852/- for Rs. 100,000/-. Per schedule of charges: Lease Processing/Documentation 0.5% on Lease Capital (Rs.2,500–Rs.12,500); Early settlement 0.5% of original capital + VAT + 10% of unearned interest; Termination fee Rs.1,500 (below Rs.1Mn) / Rs.3,000 (over Rs.1Mn).
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page)
maxFunding: Maximum up to Rs. 50 Mn
guarantee: Lease agreement, absolute ownership of the leased asset in the name of the bank
eligibilityText: Should be a registered enterprise/Professional/Person operating a registered business/Fixed income earner/high net worth individual. Should open and operate a savings account with Pradeshiya Sanwardhana Bank. Should not be a defaulter of previous financial facilities. Should be able to generate sufficient cash flow after acquiring the proposed vehicle/equipment to pay the lease rentals. Owner/partner/shareholder/director: citizen of Sri Lanka, 18–60 age group, prior experience/knowledge in the related enterprise acceptable to the bank.

useCaseText: The loan is for existing business expansion only (product page). Business expansion purpose. Working capital finance. Create employment opportunities (KFD).
description: Small Medium Enterprise loan (Small business loan) is a product which is offered to both male and female entrepreneurs. The key features of this loan are: The loan is for existing business expansion only. (KFD: Helps to provide financial services to those in remote locations where traditional financial institutions do not serve; Business expansion purpose; Working capital finance; Fast service; Flexible repayment terms.)
tenorText: Loan duration / period: 3, 6, 12, 18, 24 months. No. of instalments: 6, 11, 23, 34, 45 weeks. (KFD: Tenor 6-36 months)
costText: Annual Effective Rate of Interest: 30% and upwards (depends on type of facility, credit risk of client, tenure and security offered; revised by management from time to time). Loan Processing Fee of 3% based on finance amount. Client Verification Fee of 2% based on finance amount. Insurance: Applicable. Recovery officer visit costs and all legal/other expenses on delinquency borne by the customer.
minFunding: 30 ('000 LKR) - Loan ceiling (1st loan) LKR 30,000
maxFunding: 500 ('000 LKR) - Maximum loan limit LKR 500,000 (Follow-on loan up to LKR 500,000)
guarantee: Guarantor(s) required (Guarantors' Statement). Loan Security Fund. Individual lending via client groups without joint-liability.
eligibilityText: Applicants should be citizens of Sri Lanka. Applicant aged between 18 to 65 years (upper age will not be applicable for existing customers). Funds granted shall be utilized only for approved purposes. Facility will be approved based on evaluating Business Reports and Repayment capacity. Loan installment should be paid on weekly/bi-weekly/monthly basis. Initial Collateral savings deposit which is 5% of the loan amount and balance build up.
eligibleApplicants: Both male and female entrepreneurs operating small/medium enterprises
businessSizeText: Small Medium Enterprise; existing businesses; low-income entrepreneurs without access to credit from traditional banks
collateralText: Collateral requirement: Loan Security Fund. Initial Collateral savings deposit which is 5% of the loan amount and balance build up (KFD).
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)
capitalRequirement: Initial Collateral savings deposit of 5% of loan amount (KFD)
greenCertRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD) - not applicable, no green product

productName: All inclusive Credit Packages with CapEx & Working Capital Financing — Overdraft
useCaseText: Overdraft facility – Can be arranged at your convenience. Companies who are interested in investing their excess funds in high interest yielding instruments are supplied with money market instruments to get the maximum benefit.
description: Overdraft linked to the customer's current account; the customer uses as much as needed up to the approved limit; interest is paid only on the utilised amount. Limits can be increased/decreased through Internet banking.

costText: Interest on utilisation only; rate negotiated based on facility size and security; Temporary Overdraft charges (per General Tariff): Rs. 100 (<Rs. 4,999) up to Rs. 10,000 (>= Rs. 1 Mn); Extension of TOD 0.08% Min Rs. 1,000 Max Rs. 10,000. Credit-facility processing 0.5–0.8% (Min Rs. 500 – Max Rs. 250,000) per General Tariff §5.2.

maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, tariff)
eligibilityText: Corporates and SMEs requiring short-term working capital; sole proprietors can also apply.
collateralText: Security as acceptable to the bank; typically linked to current account and may require collateral.

capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, rates & tariff schedule)
greenCertRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, rates & tariff schedule)

useCaseText: Leasing — These 'finance leases' are available to acquire through the widest lease finance network in the country. productName: BOC Sustainability Bond Issue 2025 (Basel III Tier II)
useCaseText: Listed, rated, unsecured, subordinated, redeemable five-year Sustainability Bond, fully compliant with Basel III Tier II regulations and incorporating a non-viability write-down mechanism. The bonds will be denominated in Sri Lankan Rupees and listed on the Colombo Stock Exchange. Investors may choose between two interest options: a fixed rate of 10.50 percent per annum or a floating rate priced at two percentage points above the weighted average 12-month Treasury bill rate, reset annually. The instrument carries an issue rating of AA- (Stable) from Lanka Rating Agency, while BOC's issuer rating stands at AA-(lka)/Stable from Fitch Ratings Lanka. The minimum subscription for qualified investors is Rs. 10,000, while individual investors are required to apply for at least Rs. 5 million.
description: Basel III Tier II Sustainability Bond — 5-year, LKR-denominated, CSE-listed; 75% social use-of-proceeds (incl. MSME employment generation), 25% green (renewable energy: solar/wind/hydro/biogas; energy efficiency; green construction; clean transport; sustainable water; pollution prevention/waste). Use-of-proceeds Framework aligned to ICMA Green/Social/Sustainability Bond Principles and Sri Lanka Green Finance Taxonomy; KPMG SPO.
tenorText: 5 years
costText: Fixed 10.50% p.a. OR Floating 12-month T-bill weighted average + 200 bps, reset annually; interest paid annually.
guarantee: Basel III Tier II non-viability write-down mechanism
eligibilityText: Initial issue: 100 million bonds at Rs. 100 each (Rs. 10 bn); upsize option to 200 million bonds (Rs. 20 bn). Subscription opens 22 Dec 2025; open 14 market days unless closed earlier. Minimum subscription for qualified investors Rs. 10,000; individual investors min Rs. 5 million.
eligibleApplicants: Local and foreign qualified investors; individual investors
collateralText: Unsecured, subordinated.

useCaseText: If a Murabaha transaction is done locally with a customer for a short term, local Murabaha can be used. […] used for short/medium/long term finance for: Raw material; Inventory; Equipment; Asset financing; Vehicle financing; Or any other asset to be purchased.
description: Local Murabaha — If a Murabaha transaction is done locally with a customer for a short term, local Murabaha can be used. Murabaha is a contract wherein the bank, upon request by the customer, purchases an asset from a 3rd party supplier/vendor and resells it to the customer with an agreed mark up (cost plus profit) either against immediate payment or on a deferred payment basis. Flexible repayment plans (short term / long term).
tenorText: short/ medium/ long term finance
tenor: Long-term (> 5 years), Medium-term (1-5 years), Short-term (< 1 year)
costText: Mark up (cost plus profit) agreed at the conclusion of the contract; price cannot be changed for early or late settlement. No interest (Shariah-compliant). SME pricing: 'For SME Financing Pricings please contact the Branch Manager of Customer Relationship Manager' (Advance Pricing Nov 2025). Fees & charges per tariff schedule. Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheets)

useCaseText: Any imports-related transactions can be done through import Murabaha. […] Used for: Raw material; Inventory; Equipment; Asset financing.
description: Import Murabaha (Cost Plus Markup) — Murabaha refers to the sale of a commodity where the seller agrees with the buyer to provide him with a specific commodity (import consignment) on an agreed profit, added to his costs. Amana Bank first purchases the goods (import), gains constructive possession of it and then sells it to the customer. Salient Features: Asset backed financing; Transparent fixed pricing; Amana Bank takes full ownership and risk when buying and selling the goods; Sharia compliant; No hidden costs; No processing fee; No interest rate fluctuation risk.
tenorText: short/ medium/ long term finance
costText: Transparent fixed pricing (cost plus profit markup); No hidden costs; No processing fee; No interest rate fluctuation risk (Trade Services KFD). LC-opening commissions per Trade Services Tariff: Sight LC 1.00% first quarter + 0.125% p.m. additional quarter, min LKR 5,000; SWIFT charges LKR 3,000.

useCaseText: If the cost of a transaction can't be identified and only the selling price is disclosed, locally or internationally Musawamah can be used. Used in situations where it is not possible to compute the extra cost of goods due to: a) Arrival of shipment before receipt of shipping documents (Clearing under Shipping Guarantee); b) Usance / Acceptance where actual payment to supplier is made on a future date.
description: Import Musawamma (Lump Sum Financing) — Musawamma is a simple transaction where the seller sells his commodity without disclosing his cost. Musawamma as a Mode of Finance Amana Bank offers this product in situations where it is not possible to compute the extra cost of goods. Musawamma is a financing technique which allows to sell at a lump sum fixed price, facilitating the clearance of the goods from the port to the customer's warehouse.

useCaseText: Tijara is a Sale & Agency based financing facility for customers who sell Finished Goods on credit basis. The facility will enable the Customers to sell their Finished Goods stock, meet their working capital requirements and enjoy the benefits of cash sales. […] Tijarah Financing can be done for local sales and exports. Sub-types: 1. Tijarah Financing for Local Sales; 2. Tijarah Financing for Export.
description: Working Capital Financing for Finished Goods or Tijara is a Sale & Agency based financing facility for customers who sell Finished Goods on a credit basis. Under this concept the Bank will purchase the finished goods of the Customers and will appoint the Customer as its agent to sell the same goods in the market generally on credit basis. After receipt of shipment documents the financing converts from Pre-shipment to Post-shipment Tijarah Financing; after Acceptance, from Post-shipment to Tijarah (Bank Risk Line). Conversion allows the customer to reutilize Pre-shipment Financing.
tenorText: for Ready-to-Sale goods, either on Credit basis or on Cash basis and held in customer's inventory for longer period
costText: Price for the contract should be known at the conclusion of the contract; price can be in the form of cash or tangible goods or the usufruct of an asset. Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheets)

useCaseText: Istisna is a contract for the sale of specified items to be manufactured or constructed, with an obligation on the part of the manufacturer or builder (contractor) to deliver them to the customer upon completion. Modes of Financing: • Working Capital – For manufacturing needs. • Trade Finance – For exports and as an Islamic alternative to export Usance bill purchase. • Long-Term Finance – For construction of buildings, plants, factories, and BOT projects. • Consumer Housing Finance – For construction of apartments.
description: Working Capital Financing for Work-in-Progress or Istisna is a contract of sale of specified items to be manufactured or constructed, with an obligation on the part of the manufacturer or builder (contractor) to deliver them to the bank upon completion. Subsequently the bank would deliver the manufactured / built item to the customer. An Istisna contract is permitted only for raw materials that can be transformed from their natural state by a manufacturing or construction process involving labor.
tenorText: Working Capital – manufacturing; Trade Finance – exports; Long-Term Finance – construction, BOT; Consumer Housing Finance
tenor: Long-term (> 5 years), Medium-term (1-5 years), Short-term (< 1 year)
costText: Price for an Istisna contract be known at the conclusion of the contract. Price may be paid in advance or deferred or paid in instalments within a certain period. Price may vary in accordance with variations in delivery date. Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheets)
gracePeriod: The price of an Istisna contract may be paid in advance or deferred or paid in instalments within a certain period.

useCaseText: Asset Financing is based under the principles of Diminishing Musharaka. […] This is a general Diminishing Musharaka transaction. The Bank and the Customer jointly purchase the Asset (i.e. property/machinery etc.) and then the bank leases its share to the customer. The customer promises to buy the equity share of the bank gradually until the title to the equity is completely transferred to him.
description: Asset Financing is based under the principles of Diminishing Musharaka. Under Diminishing Musharaka, The Bank and the Customer jointly purchase the Asset (i.e. property/machinery etc.) and then the Bank leases its share to the customer. The Customer buys the equity share of the Bank gradually until the title to the equity is completely transferred to him. There will be a mandatory holding period of 12 months, during which the customer cannot purchase units of the portion of the property owned by the Bank and will only service a monthly rental, hence, the ownership of the asset between Amãna Bank and the customer will remain unchanged.
tenorText: mandatory holding period of 12 months
costText: Monthly rental during 12-month holding period; thereafter customer buys equity share gradually. Diminishing Musharaka is profit-sharing not interest. SME pricing: contact Branch Manager / CRM (Advance Pricing Nov 2025). Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheets)
gracePeriod: Mandatory holding period of 12 months during which the customer cannot purchase units of the portion of the property owned by the Bank and will only service a monthly rental.

useCaseText: Local Musharaka: If a customer wants to finance his entire business by Amãna Bank a Local Musharaka financing can be used.
description: Musharaka is a partnership in which the partners join together with different contributions, work or obligation for the common objective of undertaking business and trade in accordance with the principles of Sharia. Local Musharaka — If a customer wants to finance his entire business by Amãna Bank a Local Musharaka financing can be used. Both bank and customer contribute capital. Profit is distributed by agreed ratio; genuine losses borne pro-rata to capital. Bank acts as sleeping partner; customer is main working partner.
costText: Profit-sharing (not interest). The ratio of profit distribution must be agreed at the time of execution of the contract. The ratio of profit for each partner must be determined in proportion to the actual profit earned by the project/business and not in proportion to the capital invested. Genuine losses borne by bank and customer according to capital contribution ratio. Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheets)

useCaseText: This product is based on the principles of Ijarah where bank buys the asset (vehicles, machine, equipment, etc.) desired by the customer and rents it to him with the promise to gift it at the end of the rental period. Up to 100% of the value of the asset for 7 years can be financed.
description: Commercial and Machinery Leasing — A transaction where the bank buys the asset (vehicles, machine, equipment, etc.) desired by the customer and rents it to him with the promise to gift it at the end of the rental period. According to the lease agreement the lessor (Amãna Bank) conveys to the lessee (Customer), the right to use an asset for an agreed period of time, in return for a payment or series of payment. Rentals will be due only upon commencement of usage of the asset. Rentals will be charged only if the asset is in working condition.

productName: Trade Services — Imports under Documentary Credit (Sight & Usance LC)
useCaseText: Amana Bank Trade services are designed to facilitate your International and Domestic Trade needs with innovative and customized trade solutions. Our trade services enable us to help your needs through successfully streamlining import and export processes. Imports under Documentary Credit (Sight & Usance).
description: Imports under Documentary Credit (Sight & Usance) — Letter of Credit issued by Amana Bank in favor of an overseas supplier to secure payment for goods imported by the customer. Sight LCs require payment on presentation of documents; Usance LCs allow deferred payment after acceptance.
tenorText: Sight LC: First Quarter + Additional Quarter pricing; Usance LC: First Quarter + Additional Quarter pricing
costText: Per Trade Services Tariff (DBU 2022): LC Opening – Sight LC Commission: First Quarter 1.00%, Additional Quarter 0.125%p.m., Minimum LKR 5,000, SWIFT Charges LKR 3,000. LC Opening – Usance LC Commission: First Quarter 1.00%, Additional Quarter 0.125%p.m., Minimum LKR 5,000, SWIFT Charges LKR 3,000. Issuance of Draft LC: LKR 2,000; Non-Issuance LKR 1,000; Amendments LKR 500 each. LC Confirmation arranging LKR 2,500 each. LC Enhancement (Sight/Usance) First Quarter 1.00%, additional 0.125%p.m., min LKR 3,000. Extension: 0.125%p.m. min LKR 3,000. Retirement of Bills Sight LC LKR 2,500 commission + LKR 2,000 SWIFT; Usance LC SWIFT LKR 2,000. Acceptance Charges (Usance) First Quarter 0.30%, Additional Quarter 0.125%p.m. 25% discount for Prestige and Business Plus (Gold); 10% discount for Vantage and Expat Gold. + applicable VAT.
gracePeriod: Usance LC allows deferred payment to the supplier per LC terms (no separate grace period disclosed).

productName: Ayomi Women's Entrepreneur Loan — Structured Entrepreneurs
useCaseText: For Structured entrepreneurs : Rs. 100,000.00 – Rs. 5,000,000.00 […] Women entrepreneurs who possess valid business registration and proper management records, operate with well structured business processed, and maintain basic financial statements or financial records.
description: Ayomi loan for structured women entrepreneurs (with valid business registration, management records and basic financial statements). A strategic financial inclusion product to empower women-led micro and small businesses, covering service (education, tourism, healthcare, wellness) and manufacturing (agriculture, food processing, tailoring, handicraft) sectors. Up to 80% of project cost. Disbursement direct to supplier or customer account.
tenorText: Maximum tenure of 60 months (including grace period)
costText: Fixed 5-year rate structure: Up to Rs.1Mn — 10% (up to 1yr) / 10.5% (2–3yr) / 12% (3–5yr); Above Rs.1Mn — 11% / 11.5% / 13%. 0.25% rate reduction if a Kotipathi/Diriya (active 6 months) or Abhimani (min balance Rs.10,000, active 1yr) account maintained satisfactorily.
gracePeriod: Up to 6 months (depending on project nature)
guarantee: Collateral / guarantor based (see security)
eligibilityText: Women entrepreneurs with valid business registration and proper management records, well-structured business processes, and basic financial statements/records. Women-led business per CBSL: >50% female-owned, OR ≥20% female-owned + ≥1 female major decision-maker + ≥30% female board. Max age 65 at maturity. Valid TIN required before disbursement.
eligibleApplicants: Women-owned / led micro and small businesses (structured)
businessSizeText: For Structured entrepreneurs: Rs.100,000 – Rs.5,000,000 (micro and small level)
collateralText: Collateral: movable/immovable assets or guarantor. Up to Rs.0.5Mn — 2 guarantors; Rs.0.5–0.999Mn — min 1 guarantor & simple lodgement; ≥1Mn — mortgage.
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates table, general T&C)
capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates table, general T&C)
greenCertRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates table, general T&C)
requiredDocs: DTAP insurance (assigned to bank); valid Business Registration; minimum 1 year of business operation; financial records or management accounts; credit history review; valid TIN before disbursement.
processingTime: Within 7 working days of completed documentation (~0.25 months)
contactPerson: Agri & Micro Finance Division / nearest branch

useCaseText: A Forward Contract allows you to buy or sell one currency against another for settlement in the future at a predetermined rate and date. costText: Priced via the forward exchange rate (no separate commission published). Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, schedule of charges, lending rates table, general T&C)

useCaseText: To raise medium-term funds (2-4 Years) for a company from the capital market, typically for financing accounts receivable, inventories and meeting medium-term liabilities.
description: A loan that is backed by receivables other than mortgage loans. Securitisation is an arrangement whereby the title to a company's receivables is sold to a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV), which issues notes to investors who finance the transaction. Receivables are usually a pool of financial assets such as mortgage-backed loans or leases and credit card receivables.

Referenced by DFI as a Government-implemented MSME loan scheme; delivered through 13 licensed banks. Development targets: 20% of sub-loans to start-ups, 20% to women-led SMEs.
tenorText: Repayment: Generally ten (10) years including grace period; considering the loan repayment date of banks to government, maximum loan repayment period reduced up to 05 years
costText: Interest rate as determined by PFIs based on their borrowed rate, Administration costs, Risk coverage and possible interest margins; the interest margin of the PFI should be in accordance with the market rates. Current rate is maximum 10%
gracePeriod: Maximum 02 years
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Treasury 'Summary of MSME related Loan Schemes Implemented by the Government' PDF)
guarantee: None disclosed (working capital loans non-collateralized; PFI obtains collateral for other loans)
eligibilityText: The enterprises must be registered. Sub borrowers who are not formally registered can submit evidence that its business registration application has been accepted by the Registrar of Companies or the respective local government authority. Sub borrowers (the parent company and any subsidiaries) must have annual turnover less than Rs. 1,000 million and less than 200 employees.
eligibleApplicants: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) in all sectors except trading, leasing and renting
businessSizeText: Eligible Categories: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs)
collateralText: (i) Working Capital Loans should be non-collateralized. (ii) PFI should obtain suitable collateral for other loans to minimize the risk involved in disbursement and recovery of loans.
turnoverRequirement: annual turnover less than Rs. 1,000 million and less than 200 employees

institution: Department of Financial Inclusion, Central Bank of Sri Lanka
productName: Stimulating Package for Re-energizing the MSME Sector in Sri Lanka — Component II: MSMEs in the NPL Category
useCaseText: Working capital loans for MSMEs in operational but classified as Non-performing borrowers
description: Government of Sri Lanka (GoSL) funded working-capital component for MSMEs in operational but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettled existing loans resulting from the recent economic downturn (fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded). Referenced by DFI as a Government-implemented MSME loan scheme; delivered through 15 licensed banks.
tenorText: Repayment: Maximum of 5 years including the grace period
costText: Maximum 8%
gracePeriod: Maximum of 6 months
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Treasury 'Summary of MSME related Loan Schemes Implemented by the Government' PDF)
guarantee: None disclosed (PFIs should obtain required securities by revaluating existing collateralized assets)
eligibilityText: In addition to the eligibility criteria mentioned in clauses Loan Component I, the following should also be applicable for component II of the loan scheme: (i) MSMEs that are in operational level but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettlement of existing loans as a result of the recent economic downturn are eligible for obtaining loans under this component. Fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded. (ii) Priority should be given to persons / MSMEs which became NPL due to being a guarantor for other personal / business loans. (iii) Wilful defaulters, defaults due to the diversion of funds, defaults due to frauds should not be considered.
eligibleApplicants: MSMEs in operational but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers (closed-down MSMEs excluded)
businessSizeText: Eligible Categories: MSMEs in operational but classified as Non-performing borrowers
collateralText: PFIs should obtain required securities to mitigate the risk by revaluating the existing assets that were collateralized to the bank by the respective sub-borrower or in cases where the reevaluated assets are insufficient to cover the risk of the new loan, the respective bank […].
turnoverRequirement: annual turnover less than Rs. 1,000 million and less than 200 employees (per Component I criteria)

productName: Global Trade Accelerator Support (GTAS) Scheme — Participation in Trade Fairs / Exhibitions
useCaseText: Participation in Trade Fairs/Exhibitions. Assistance will cover space rent for maximum sq.mt area, Construction of stand, Rental of furniture and equipment, listing in official exhibition directory, Registration Fees/Application Fee, Cost of electricity, Exhibition insurance, web promotion, media entry and participation cost of virtual trade fairs.
description: Category 1 (Export Market Promotion): Assistance up to a maximum Rs. 550,000/- per company per year for participation in Trade Fairs/Exhibitions. The trade fair/exhibition selected should be acceptable to the EDB in terms of usefulness to generate additional exports; applicant must participate under its own name with at least one delegate.
tenorText: Implemented from July 2025 to November 2025 (GTAS 2025); GTAS Phase II implemented January to November.
costText: Cost-sharing reimbursement basis; maximum Rs. 550,000/- per company per year (combined Category 1 ceiling shared with certifications).
gracePeriod: Not applicable (reimbursement scheme)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: GTAS guidelines)
maxFunding: 550 ('000 LKR) per company per year (Category 1 ceiling)
eligibilityText: All exporters & potential exporters of products or services, having local ownership of 51% or more. The trade fair/exhibition selected by the company should be acceptable to the EDB in terms of its usefulness to generate additional exports.
eligibleApplicants: Exporters & potential exporters of products or services with local ownership of 51% or more
businessSizeText: All exporters & potential exporters of products or services, having local ownership of 51% or more are eligible to apply under this scheme.
collateralText: Not applicable (cost-sharing reimbursement grant)
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: GTAS guidelines)
capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: GTAS guidelines)

productName: Assistance Scheme to Facilitate SMEs to Obtain Standards / Certifications Required Internationally
useCaseText: To assist SME exporters and export-oriented SME companies registered under the EDB New Exporter Development Programme in obtaining standards/certifications required to access international markets; EDB reimburses the cost of certification and consultancy fees.
description: The EDB implements a Financial Assistance Scheme to support SME exporters and export-oriented SME companies registered under the EDB New Exporter Development Programme in obtaining standards/certifications required to access international markets. EDB will reimburse eligible beneficiaries for the cost of certification and consultancy fees up to a maximum of Rs. 0.3 million per company on reimbursement basis. Eligible certifications include ISO 9001 (QMS), ISO/IEC 27001 (ISMS), SOC 2, GDPR, ISO 20000, PCI-DSS, FDA, CE Mark, etc. Renewal of existing certifications is not eligible.
tenorText: The Assistance Scheme shall remain in operation for a period of one (1) year, with effect from the final date for the receipt of applications.
costText: Cost-sharing reimbursement basis; EDB will reimburse the cost of certification and consultancy fees up to a maximum of Rs. 0.3 million per company. Renewal of existing certifications is not eligible.
gracePeriod: Not applicable (reimbursement scheme)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: scheme guidelines)
maxFunding: 300 ('000 LKR) per company
eligibilityText: SME Exporters / Export-oriented SME companies registered under the EDB New Exporter Development Programme. Eligible product sectors: Processed Food and Beverages, Spices and Concentrates, Fisheries, Electrical and Electronic Components, Ayurveda Sector, Personal Protective Equipment, Life Style Products, Chemical Based Products, ICT/BPM Sector, etc.
eligibleApplicants: SME exporters / export-oriented SME companies registered under the EDB New Exporter Development Programme
businessSizeText: SME Exporters/ Export oriented SME companies registered under the EDB New Exporter Development Programme.
valueChainText: Eligible product sectors listed: Processed Food and Beverages, Spices and Concentrates, Fisheries, Electrical and Electronic Components, Ayurveda Sector, Personal Protective Equipment, Life Style Products, Chemical Based Products, ICT/BPM Sector, etc.

capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: scheme guidelines)

smeApplicabilityText: UNCLEAR: eligible product sectors list does not explicitly name apparel/textiles, but includes "Life Style Products" and "Personal Protective Equipment" and "etc."; T&C applicability not explicitly confirmed

productName: Erambuma Credit Scheme – Financial Support for Innovative Business Start-Ups (Enterprise Sri Lanka Programme)
useCaseText: Financial support for innovative business start-ups by Sri Lankan graduates, diploma, higher diploma and graduate-level skill certificate holders, to commercialize and accelerate growth. Both investment and working capital loans are considered (working capital funding should not exceed 50% of the total facility).
description: The Erambuma Credit Scheme (National Budget 2018, Enterprise Sri Lanka Programme) provides financial support for innovative business start-ups by Sri Lankan graduates, diploma, higher diploma and graduate-level skill certificate holders. The loan is provided on the merit of a business plan and a viable business idea, operated islandwide. Both investment and working capital loans are considered; working capital funding should not exceed 50% of the total facility. Priority given to business ideas related to the export value chain.
tenorText: The Applicant shall be liable to pay back the total amount of the facility borrowed from the bank within seven years from the date of disbursement.
costText: The interest rate will be 100% subsidized by the Government. Loans granted on non-collateral basis through Bank of Ceylon & People's Bank.
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: Erambuma scheme document)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Erambuma scheme document)
maxFunding: 1,500 ('000 LKR) per person per company per innovative idea (Rs. 1.5 million)
guarantee: Loans granted on non-collateral basis (Government interest subsidy); guarantee via the scheme through Bank of Ceylon & People's Bank.
eligibilityText: A citizen of Sri Lanka below 35 years; young university Graduate or certificate holder of NVQ level 5, 6 & 7; not a defaulter to any Bank or financial institution; has a suitable project location; able to submit a business plan with positive cash flow; has required knowledge and skills. Applicant should not be a Government employee, a citizen of another country or a person living abroad.
eligibleApplicants: Sri Lankan graduates, diploma/higher diploma and NVQ level 5/6/7 certificate holders (individuals/incubators/companies) below 35 years
businessSizeText: Business start-ups (innovative) set up by Sri Lankan graduates, diploma, higher diploma and graduate-level skill certificate holders.
valueChainText: The priority will be given for business ideas related to export value chain.
collateralText: Loans will be granted on non-collateral basis through Bank of Ceylon & People's Bank.
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Erambuma scheme document)
capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Erambuma scheme document)
requiredDocs: Online application (from http://exporter.edb.gov.lk); business plan with positive cash flow; merit of a viable business idea (evaluated by EDB committee).
processingTime: Not disclosed (sources checked: Erambuma scheme document)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: "The priority will be given for business ideas related to export value chain" — export-oriented start-ups including T&C eligible; restricted to graduates/diploma holders below 35

useCaseText: To facilitate exporters / potential exporters to differentiate their products in the niche export market by obtaining the Fair Trade Certification (both provisional and full memberships).
description: The EDB facilitates exporters / potential exporters to differentiate their products in the niche export market by obtaining Fair Trade Certification. Assistance on reimbursement and cost-sharing basis: EDB will reimburse 60% of the total cost of obtaining the Fair Trade certification through a recognized Fair Trade Authority up to a maximum of Rs. 200,000 per company. Extended for both provisional and full memberships of Fair Trade certification within the maximum amount.

productName: Scheme to Assist SME Apparel Companies Manufacturing / Exporting Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) in Obtaining Product and Production Facility Standards / Certifications Required Internationally
useCaseText: To assist SME Apparel companies manufacturing and exporting Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) to obtain ISO standard for the production facilities (ISO 13485) and CE certification for products required by international buyers.
description: The EDB implements a financial scheme to assist SME Apparel companies manufacturing and exporting Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) in obtaining product and production facility standards/certifications required by international buyers. The scheme facilitates obtaining ISO 13485 for production facilities and CE certification for products. EDB will reimburse 50% of the total cost of obtaining both ISO 13485 and CE certification, up to a maximum of Rs. 1.5 Mn per company. Companies may obtain assistance for both certifications either together or separately within the two-year period.
tenorText: The assistance scheme will be in operation for a period of two years with effect from the date of circulation of the advertisement.
costText: Cost-sharing reimbursement basis; EDB will reimburse 50% of the total cost of obtaining both ISO 13485 and CE certification, up to a maximum of Rs. 1.5 Mn per company, on submission of relevant documents.

maxFunding: 1,500 ('000 LKR) per company (Rs. 1.5 Mn)

useCaseText: This initiative is designed to provide financial assistance to small and medium-scale industrial enterprises, helping them expand operations, improve productivity, and invest in business development.
description: The Industrial Development Board (IDB) supports the growth and sustainability of Sri Lanka's industrial sector through the "Industry" Loan Scheme, introduced in 2023, providing affordable financing to eligible industrial entrepreneurs. Implemented in partnership with the Regional Development Bank (RDB) and funded from the Industrial Development Fund. Financing covers machinery purchases and working capital for manufacturing/production-based industries. Includes a women-entrepreneur cohort delivered with WCIC and Unilever.

costText: Interest Rate 6% per annum (subsidised). No other fees disclosed.

eligibilityText: Operate a manufacturing or production-based industry in operation more than one year; be registered with the IDB; submit a valid Business Registration Certificate; provide a statement of business income and expenditure; submit machinery quotations (for machinery) or a detailed cost estimate (for working capital); provide a copy of the applicant's NIC; be able to provide the required guarantor or security arrangements; not be listed as a defaulter in the Credit Information Bureau (CRIB).

Eligible sectors: MSMEs in operational but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettled existing loans resulting from the recent economic downturn (Fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded). Implementing licensed banks: Bank of Ceylon, People's Bank, Regional Development Bank, Hatton National Bank, Seylan Bank PLC, Sampath Bank PLC, Commercial Bank of Ceylon PLC, DFCC Bank, National Development Bank, Nations Trust Bank, Sanasa Development Bank PLC, Union Bank of Colombo PLC, Pan Asia Banking Corporation PLC, State Mortgage & Investment Bank, Cargills Bank.

eligibilityText: In addition to the eligibility criteria mentioned in clause Loan Component I, the following should also be applicable for component II of the loan scheme: (i) MSMEs that are in operational level but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettlement of existing loans as a results of the recent economic downturn are eligible for obtaining loans under this component. Fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded. (ii) Priority should be given to persons / MSMEs which became NPL due to being a guarantor for other personal / business loans. (iii) Willful defaulters, defaults due to the diversion of funds, defaults due to frauds should not be considered.

institution: Ministry of Finance, Planning and Economic Development (The Treasury of Sri Lanka)
productName: Special Working Capital Loan Scheme to strengthen the Tourism Industry affected by the Easter Sunday Terrorist Attacks
useCaseText: Fulfill the working capital requirement of the target beneficiaries.
description: The Government introduced a “Relief Package” to the tourism sector under the Enterprise Sri Lanka Programme, to mitigate impacts on the tourism industry due to the Easter Sunday terrorist attacks. As per Cabinet decisions of 07.05.2019, tourism-sector institutions or individuals can obtain a fresh Working Capital (W/C) loan to recover and strengthen their capacity. Implementation commenced 07 May 2019.
tenorText: The maximum tenor of the loan is two (02) years.
costText: Effective rate of interest is 13.86% p.a. (during 2019). Interest subsidy borne by the Government is 10.40% p.a.; interest rate applicable for the borrower is 3.46% p.a. (during 2019). Rate revised by the DFD at end of December each year. In the event of reschedulement, interest subsidy shall not be borne by the Government.
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: Tourism W/C scheme PDF)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Tourism W/C scheme PDF)
maxFunding: Tiered by annual turnover: Turnover ≤ Rs.100Mn → max Rs.20Mn; >100–250Mn → Rs.50Mn; >250–750Mn → Rs.150Mn; >750Mn → Rs.250Mn ('000 LKR: 20,000 / 50,000 / 150,000 / 250,000). An institution can obtain only one loan under this scheme.
guarantee: None disclosed
eligibilityText: 3.1 Individuals and institutions providing services to tourism sector who are registered with any of the following institutions: a) Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority b) Sri Lanka Tourism Promotion Bureau c) Sri Lanka Tourism Convention Bureau d) Sri Lanka Institute of Tourism and Hotel Management e) The Hotels Association of Sri Lanka. 3.2 Individuals and institutions providing services to tourism sector who are having a valid business registration.
eligibleApplicants: Individuals and institutions providing services to the tourism sector
businessSizeText: Based on existing annual turnover tiers up to >Rs.750 million

turnoverRequirement: Maximum of one year working capital requirement considered, based on existing annual turnover tiers

institution: Ministry of Finance, Planning and Economic Development (The Treasury of Sri Lanka)
productName: Jaya Isura Loan Scheme (Enterprise Sri Lanka)
useCaseText: Empower the small and medium scale enterprises including agriculture, fisheries, livestock, floriculture, horticulture, light engineering, printing, tourism, handicrafts, garments, information technology, manufacturing industry and renewable energy sectors based on their trade turnover and to increase the number of job opportunities created.
description: Interest Subsidy Loan Scheme to empower SMEs across many sectors including garments/apparel, IT, manufacturing and renewable energy. Part of the Enterprise Sri Lanka programme (2017–2019), a closed/historical interest-subsidy programme. Category I: Small enterprises (turnover Rs.10–250M, 5–50 employees) – max Rs.100M (export) / Rs.50M (non-export), 50% subsidy, borrower 6.54%/onlending 6.75%. Category II: Medium entrepreneurs (turnover Rs.250–750M, 51–300 employees) – max Rs.400M (export) / Rs.200M (non-export), 25% subsidy, borrower 4.81%/onlending 10.12%.
tenorText: Loan recovery/repayment period of 5 years
costText: Annual Effective Interest Rate 13.5%. Category I: 50% interest subsidy, borrower rate 6.54% (onlending 6.75%). Category II: 25% interest subsidy, borrower rate 4.81% (onlending 10.12%).

Category II: Medium entrepreneurs with an annual turnover between Rs. 250 million to Rs. 750 million and with an employment cadre of 51 to 300 engaged in the above sectors.

institution: Ministry of Finance, Planning and Economic Development (The Treasury of Sri Lanka)
productName: National Credit Guarantee Institution (NCGI) — Credit Guarantee Facility
useCaseText: Supporting the small and medium enterprise (SME) sector by assisting SMEs in overcoming obstacles such as an inability to adapt to rapidly changing market demands, changing technology, and limitations in capacity due to a low level of financial inclusion, limited access to finance, and esoteric regulations.
description: National Credit Guarantee Institution (NCGI), incorporated April 2021 as a public limited company (reg. P B 00271461). A credit-guarantee facility to support SME access to finance. Guarantee coverage up to 2/3 of the loan (67%) on a pari-passu basis; maximum guarantee limit 5 times the total investment. Funded with ADB USD 100 million government contribution; PFIs USD 11 million; LBs USD 10 million; NBFIs USD 0.75 million. Ownership 90 (Gov.) : 10 (PFIs). Revenue model: Investment Income + Guarantee Fee Income + Government Subsidy.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: NCGI document)
costText: Annual Premium 1–2% (guarantee fee). Guarantee Coverage: up to 2/3 of the loan (67%), pari-passu basis. Maximum Guarantee Limit: 5 times the total investment.

productName: Individual Guarantee Scheme — Standard Guarantee Scheme
useCaseText: Only term loans (LKR) to fund CAPEX and Permanent Working Capital.
description: NCGIL support Sri Lankan MSMEs by providing partial credit guarantees to the participating shareholder financial institutions (Banks and FIs) to encourage uncollateralized lending based on viability and cash flows of Sri Lankan businesses. Standard Guarantee Scheme: guarantee covers 67% of the loan amount approved by the SFI.
tenorText: Capex Loan: Maximum Period: 10 Years, Minimum Period: 1 Year. Permanent WC Loan: Maximum Period: 3 Years, Minimum Period: 1 Year.
costText: Guarantee fee 1% to 2% per annum, calculated on the guaranteed amount, based on the risk of relevant business. Fees to be paid by MSME. Recovered annually on the outstanding (Guaranteed amount). [Borne by the MSME in addition to the SFI's bank interest.]
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, faq.html, app_process.html, priority_sec.html)
maxFunding: 25,000 ('000 LKR) [LKR 25 Million]
guarantee: Guarantees to the SFIs to partly secure the term loans granted to individual MSMEs. Extent of the Guarantee: 67% of the loan amount approved by the SFI (Standard Guarantee Scheme).
eligibilityText: MSMEs engaged in any business sector in agriculture / Industry / Service of the economy. Any business in Sri Lanka falling under the definition of MSME is eligible to apply for a loan from any of the shareholding financial institutions if the business is financially viable.
eligibleApplicants: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Sri Lanka
businessSizeText: MSMEs (Manufacturing & Service: Medium Rs.351Mn–1Bn; Small Rs.21–350Mn; Micro ≤ Rs.20Mn).
valueChainText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, priority_sec.html) — scheme is sector-agnostic across agriculture/industry/service; Export is a priority sector
collateralText: NCGIL provides partial credit guarantees to the participating shareholder financial institutions (Banks and FIs) to encourage uncollateralized lending based on viability and cash flows of Sri Lankan businesses.
turnoverRequirement: Per MSME classification: Micro ≤ Rs.20Mn; Small Rs.21–350Mn; Medium Rs.351Mn–1Bn annual turnover (Manufacturing & Service).
capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, faq.html, app_process.html, priority_sec.html)
greenCertRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, priority_sec.html) — environment-friendly business is a priority sector but no certification mandate disclosed
requiredDocs: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, app_process.html) — NCGIL does not lend directly; the Shareholder Financial Institution evaluates and documents the loan, then applies to NCGIL for guarantee cover
applicationForm: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, app_process.html) — MSMEs apply through any of the 13 Shareholder Financial Institutions, not NCGIL directly
processingTime: Not disclosed (sources checked: product.html, faq.html, app_process.html, priority_sec.html)
contactPerson: Himansu Wickramasinghe, Head of Guarantee Administration
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: “MSMEs engaged in any business sector in agriculture / Industry / Service of the economy”; export industry is a named priority sector, so T&C/apparel SMEs are eligible.

productName: Individual Guarantee Scheme — Liya Shakthi Guarantee Scheme
useCaseText: Only term loans (LKR) to fund CAPEX and Permanent Working Capital.
description: Liya Shakthi Guarantee Scheme is the women-owned business variant of NCGIL's partial credit guarantee, encouraging SFIs to provide uncollateralized lending to women-owned/managed MSMEs. The guarantee covers 80% of the loan amount approved by the SFI.
tenorText: Capex Loan: Maximum Period: 10 Years, Minimum Period: 1 Year. Permanent WC Loan: Maximum Period: 3 Years, Minimum Period: 1 Year.
costText: Guarantee fee 1% to 2% per annum, calculated on the guaranteed amount, based on the risk of relevant business. Fees to be paid by MSME. Recovered annually on the outstanding (Guaranteed amount). [Borne by the MSME in addition to the SFI's bank interest.]

maxFunding: 25,000 ('000 LKR) [LKR 25 Million]
guarantee: Guarantees to the SFIs to partly secure the term loans granted to individual MSMEs. Extent of the Guarantee: 80% of the loan amount approved by the SFI (Liya Shakthi Guarantee Scheme) - Women Owned Business.
eligibilityText: Women-Owned / Managed Business. A business qualifies as woman-owned/led if: (a) >50% owned by females, OR (b) >20% owned by females AND has >1 female as a major decision-maker, has >30% of the Board comprising females where a Board exists. Must fall under the MSME definition and be financially viable.

productName: Government Funded Loan Scheme (Stimulating Package for Re-energizing the MSME Sector) — Component I: Strengthening the Existing MSMEs and Start-ups
useCaseText: Investment Loans only. […] Introduce new or advance technologies for business upgrading. New constructions / expansions / refurbishment of a factory, building, warehouse, and tourism sector related locations. Vehicle purchase is not allowed unless it is a land vehicle or sub-borrowers are in the hotels or tourism sectors.
description: This project is for the purpose of providing concessionary credit facilities to eligible Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprise (MSME) sub-borrowers through selected Participatory Financial Institutions (PFIs) i.e. Licensed Commercial Banks (LCBs) and Licensed Specialized Banks (LSBs) at affordable interest rates with the objective of assisting existing MSMEs to survive, recover and expand and to provide seed capital for new MSMEs to commence business. [Component I: strengthening MSMEs that survived in the crisis and moved.] Total Fund Allocation: Rs. 13 billion.
tenorText: Repayment Period: Maximum of 10 years including the grace period
costText: The Maximum On-lending Rate to End-borrower: 7 percent per annum. (Effective Rate to PFIs: 3 percent per annum.) Interest computed on the basis of a 360 days year of twelve thirty-day months.
gracePeriod: Grace Period: Maximum of 12 months commencing from the date of disbursement of the first installment, if requested
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline (OG-Signed); SMILE-E-FRIEND-Loan-Schemes.pdf; product page — only a maximum loan limit is specified)
maxFunding: 15,000 ('000 LKR) — Maximum Rs. 15 million per MSME (one loan per entity/individual; one loan per group of companies)
guarantee: Refinance: 100 percent of the loan amount disbursed by PFIs (subject to the per-MSME upper limit). Credit risk borne by the PFI; collateral as determined by the PFI.
eligibilityText: Be a citizen in Sri Lanka. Be able to satisfy the PFI that the proposed project has a sound cash flow, a good repayment capacity and high contribution to economic revival. Be able to provide a proper business plan, with potential to growth. Be able to provide collateral if any, as determined by the PFI. A borrower should be 65 years or below. Applicant should not be a citizen of another country or a person living abroad. Multiple loans cannot be obtained; loan proceeds cannot be used to settle existing loans.
eligibleApplicants: Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (MSMEs) associated with manufacturing, construction, export oriented, tourism, apparel, fisheries, animal husbandry, agriculture and agro based industries; existing MSMEs and start-ups.
businessSizeText: Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprise (MSME) sub-borrowers
valueChainText: Eligible beneficiaries include MSMEs associated with apparel (among manufacturing, construction, export oriented, tourism, fisheries, animal husbandry, agriculture and agro based industries).
collateralText: Be able to provide collateral if any, as determined by the PFI.
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline; product page)
capitalRequirement: Equity Contribution by MSME: Minimum of 10 percent of the total estimated cost of the respective project in cash or any kind of asset (Bank Loan: Maximum of 90 percent of the estimated cost).
greenCertRequirement: In the event that the product or enterprise undergoes environmental screening, the MSME must obtain the necessary clearances from the relevant authorities (verified by the PFI prior to loan registration).
requiredDocs: Application form (Annex I) with descriptive project / business proposal; business registration; comprehensive project report. Initial selection via NEDA / Small Enterprise Development Division (SED) / Vidatha Unit / Industrial Development Board (IDB) or Development Officers of the Ministry of Industries; recommendation letter then application to the selected PFI.
processingTime: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline; product page)
contactPerson: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page; Operating Guideline — administered via NEDA / Ministry of Industries regional offices and PFIs)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: 'MSMEs associated with manufacturing, construction, export oriented, tourism, apparel, fisheries, animal husbandry, agriculture and agro based industries' — apparel explicitly eligible.

productName: Government Funded Loan Scheme (Stimulating Package for Re-energizing the MSME Sector) — Component II: MSMEs in the NPL Category
useCaseText: Working Capital Loans only. Beneficiaries are eligible to obtain working capital for their existing business.
description: Concessionary credit facilities to eligible MSME sub-borrowers through selected PFIs. Component II supports MSMEs in the NPL category — MSMEs that are in operational level but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettlement of existing loans as a result of the recent economic downturn. Fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded. Total Fund Allocation: Rs. 5 billion.
tenorText: Repayment Period: Maximum of 5 years including the grace period
costText: The Maximum On-lending Rate to End-borrower: 8 percent per annum. (Effective Rate to PFIs: 4 percent per annum.) Banks are allowed to increase the interest rate to the market rate if the borrower does not settle the loan within its original tenure; on fund misuse the bank can convert the loan to a higher interest rate or apply a higher penalty (with notice to DFD).
gracePeriod: Grace Period: Maximum of 6 months commencing from the date of disbursement, if requested
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline (OG-Signed) — only a maximum loan limit is specified)
maxFunding: 5,000 ('000 LKR) — Maximum Rs. 5 million per MSME (one loan per entity/individual)
guarantee: Refinance: 100 percent of the loan amount disbursed by PFIs (subject to the per-MSME upper limit). Bank Loan: Maximum of 100 percent of the estimated cost.
eligibilityText: In addition to the Component I criteria (citizen of Sri Lanka; sound cash flow; business plan; 65 years or below; collateral if any): MSMEs that are in operational level but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers due to unsettlement of existing loans as a result of the recent economic downturn are eligible; fully closed-down MSMEs are excluded. Priority to persons/MSMEs which became NPL due to being a guarantor for other personal/business loans. Willful defaulters, defaults due to diversion of funds or frauds are not considered.
eligibleApplicants: MSMEs (manufacturing, construction, export oriented, tourism, apparel, fisheries, animal husbandry, agriculture and agro based industries — same as Component I) that are operational but classified as Non-Performing Borrowers.
businessSizeText: Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprise (MSME) sub-borrowers in the NPL category
valueChainText: Eligible beneficiaries (same as clause 6.3) include MSMEs associated with apparel.
collateralText: PFIs should obtain required securities to mitigate the risk by revaluating the existing assets that were collateralized to the bank by the respective sub-borrower or, where insufficient, obtain new guarantees/assets as collateral to cover the risk of the new loan.
turnoverRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline; product page)
capitalRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline — no equity-contribution minimum stated for Component II (Bank Loan up to 100% of estimated cost))
greenCertRequirement: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline — environmental screening clearances required only where applicable (per Component I clause 6.2.9))
requiredDocs: Application form (Annex I) with descriptive project / business proposal; business registration. Initial selection via NEDA / SED / Vidatha Unit / IDB or Development Officers of the Ministry of Industries; recommendation letter then application to the selected PFI.
processingTime: Not disclosed (sources checked: Operating Guideline; product page)
contactPerson: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page; Operating Guideline — administered via NEDA / Ministry of Industries regional offices and PFIs)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: eligible beneficiaries 'Same as the clause No. 6.3' which lists apparel among eligible industries; restricted to operational MSMEs in the NPL category.

productName: SMILE III (Revolving Fund) Loan Scheme — General Loan (GL)
useCaseText: This loan can be used for purchasing machinery, working capital requirements, and technical assistance (consultancy services). [General Loan scheme.]
description: Under this, loan facilities are provided at a concessionary interest rate through credit institutions involved for Small and Medium scale Entrepreneurs in the manufacturing industry. This loan can be used for purchasing machinery, working capital requirements, and technical assistance (consultancy services). Allocation for the year 2023: Rs. 02 billion. Credit institutions involved: Bank of Ceylon, People's Bank, Regional Development Bank (RDB), National Development Bank (NDB), DFCC Bank, Sampath Bank, Seylan Bank, Hatton National Bank (HNB), Commercial Bank, Sanasa Development Bank (SDB).

productName: E-Friends II (Revolving Fund) Loan Scheme — General Loan (GL)
useCaseText: Credit facilities are provided for machinery and treatment plant construction as per the project operating instructions. [For projects that […] prevent waste of resources, reduce energy use and produce environmentally friendly alternative products instead of harmful products.]
description: Under this loan scheme, entrepreneurs will be given loans at concessionary interest rates for projects that cause environmental problems such as environmental pollution due to industrial production processes, prevent waste of resources, reduce energy use and produce environmentally friendly alternative products instead of harmful products. General loan scheme: credit facilities for machinery and treatment plant construction. Allocation for the year 2023: Rs. 01 billion. Credit institutions involved: Bank of Ceylon, People's Bank, Regional Development Bank (RDB), National Development Bank (NDB), DFCC Bank, Sampath Bank, Seylan Bank, Hatton National Bank (HNB), Commercial Bank, LOLC Finance, People's Leasing & Finance PLC.

eligibilityText: Under this loan scheme, entrepreneurs will be given loans at concessionary interest rates for projects that cause environmental problems such as environmental pollution due to industrial production processes, prevent waste of resources, reduce energy use and produce environmentally friendly alternative products instead of harmful products. Priority will be given to entrepreneurs operating in the manufacturing industry. The final decision regarding collaterals will be taken by the respective banks. There is no deadline for the submission of applications. A business can apply for both the loans (as a series).
eligibleApplicants: Entrepreneurs (priority to the manufacturing industry) undertaking environmentally-focused projects — pollution control, resource/energy efficiency, environmentally friendly alternative products.
valueChainText: Not disclosed (sources checked: SMILE-E-FRIEND-Loan-Schemes.pdf — environmental projects in the manufacturing industry; textile & clothing not separately named)

useCaseText: "Non-motor insurance is to provide risk management for all types of Fire, Marine and Miscellaneous Insurance." Covers fire and allied perils for property/assets.
description: Fire Insurance — covers loss or damage to property/assets from fire and allied perils. Part of NITF's non-motor general insurance offering for government bodies and the general public.
tenorText: Annual policy (general insurance), renewable. Not separately specified per line.
costText: Premium is risk-rated/quoted on underwriting; NITF publishes no tariff schedule. Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, NITF schedule of charges (none published), linked PDFs/circulars, NITF Act No.28/2006)
