useCaseText: We offer working capital solutions in the form of overdrafts on a revolving basis for a defined period of time to help businesses finance the day-to-day operations.
description: "A facility granted for current account users such that debits are permitted up to a certain threshold even if funds are not available. This will facilitate day to day working capital requirements or any urgent financial needs of your business" (KFD 2026 p.41). Overdrafts on a revolving basis for a defined period of time.
tenorText: Revolving on a defined period
costText: Loan Processing Fee — New 0.30% (Min LKR 16,500 / USD 66); Enhancement 0.275% on enhanced amount; Renewals LKR 5,000–25,000 flat depending on facility size (SME Tariff 26/01/2026, items 1.01–1.02). Penal interest 2% p.a. above normal rate on arrears (SME Tariff 2.01). Interest rate not published; market-determined.
gracePeriod: Not applicable (revolving)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, SME tariff, KFD 2026)
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, SME tariff, KFD 2026)
eligibilityText: Current account users; SME businesses (covers Manufacturing, Power, Transportation and Professional services per SME Banking page).
eligibleApplicants: SME businesses with a current account
businessSizeText: Small & Medium Enterprises (SME) – Manufacturing, Power, Transportation and Professional services
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis (KFD 2026 general SME practice)
requiredDocs: Not disclosed online; contact Relationship Manager / SME Assets Center (sources checked: product page, KFD 2026)
applicationForm: Not disclosed (sources checked: application-forms page, product page)
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: SME Banking covers "diverse industries including, Manufacturing… and Professional services" — apparel manufacturing SME is eligible.

productName: Working Capital Solutions (SME) — Short Term Loan
useCaseText: We provide flexible solutions based on your specific requirements to protect your business cash flow, help your business increase its working capital, expand, diversify, buy new fixed assets, businesses and acquire new property or technology.
description: "Short term loans are offered at competitive rates to bridge working capital shortfalls or facilitate urgent cash needs. These loans generally do not exceed 12 months" (KFD 2026 p.41).
tenorText: Generally do not exceed 12 months (KFD 2026)
costText: Processing Fee 0.30% New / 0.275% Enhancement (Min LKR 16,500 / USD 66) per SME Tariff item 1.01–1.02; Penal interest 2% p.a. above normal on arrears; Early settlement fee 3% (part) / 5% (full); interest at competitive rates, not published.
eligibilityText: SME businesses
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis
requiredDocs: Not disclosed online; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: SME Banking covers manufacturing including apparel.

productName: Working Capital Solutions (SME) — Corporate Debt Papers (Commercial Papers / FRN)
useCaseText: Corporate debt papers (Commercial papers, fixed / floating rate notes, and supplier finance based products including documentary credit and bank guarantees).
description: "Corporate debt papers (Commercial papers, fixed / floating rate notes, and supplier finance based products including documentary credit and bank guarantees)" (SME Working Capital Solutions page).
costText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, SME tariff, KFD 2026 — instrument-specific commission not published)
eligibilityText: SME businesses with established credit standing
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis
requiredDocs: Not disclosed; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: available to SMEs with adequate credit standing.

productName: Trade Finance (SME) — Import Letter of Credit (Sight & Usance)
useCaseText: This is to facilitate either the import or local purchase of raw materials or finished goods or equipment, vehicles etc., for your business requirements. Commitments under these L/Cs could be post-financed in the following manner.
description: "Irrevocable L/Cs (both on sight and usance terms) — This is to facilitate either the import or local purchase of raw materials or finished goods or equipment, vehicles etc., for your business requirements" (KFD 2026 p.42). Provides Import Letters of Credit and post-LC import financing.
tenorText: Sight or Usance terms (Usance LCs typically up to 180 days)
costText: Import Sight D/Cs: Local 2.0% per quarter (Min LKR 10,000), Foreign 0.25% (Min USD 50); 0.25% p.m. (LKR) / 0.125% p.m. (FCY) for each additional month. Import Usance D/Cs: 2.0% per quarter (Local, Min LKR 7,500) / 0.25% per quarter (FCY, Min USD 50). Confirmation/Financing Arrangement Fee USD 50 (Trade Tariff items 1.01–1.04). LC Amendments — Value enhancement Sight 0.5%/qtr (Min LKR 3,000), Usance 0.375%/qtr; Extension 0.25% p.m.; Other amendments LKR 2,500/USD 25 (item 1.06). SWIFT charges LKR 3,000 / USD 30 per message. Discrepancy fee USD 75.
guarantee: LC itself is a payment guarantee instrument issued in favour of the supplier.
eligibilityText: Importers / SMEs requiring trade payment facilitation
valueChainText: Not disclosed (applicable to all import-dependent T&C SMEs e.g. fabric/inputs sourcing)
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis; cash margin commonly required; T&C of facilities apply
requiredDocs: LC application form, performa invoice/sales contract, importer business documents (sources checked: product page, Application Forms page — no specific LC form publicly listed)
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to T&C SMEs importing raw materials, fabric, accessories, machinery.

useCaseText: Shipping Guarantees.
description: "An indemnity is given by the customer (countersigned by Union Bank) to a shipping company or its agent, to allow the shipping company to release the goods to the customer without the presentation of the original Bill of Lading. Shipping Guarantee facility will usually be a part of the suite of Import Credit offered to a customer" (KFD 2026 p.42).
tenorText: Until receipt of original B/L
costText: Shipping Guarantee (Trade Tariff 1.20): Commission for 1st month 0.50% (LKR) / 0.25% (FCY); Min LKR 5,000 / USD 40; Commission beyond 1 month (Overdue) LKR 4,000 p.m. / USD 40 p.m. Release of Import Documents free of payment LKR 5,000 / USD 50.
guarantee: Bank indemnity issued to the shipping line.
eligibilityText: Existing Import Credit customers (typically issued in conjunction with an Import LC)
eligibleApplicants: SME importers with established trade facilities
collateralText: Linked to underlying Import Credit / LC margin
requiredDocs: Not disclosed; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to T&C importers.

useCaseText: Inward Bills Collection.
description: "Collection Bills (DP/DA): This product involves Union Bank with handling of inward documents (domestic or foreign) received from a Remitting Bank and/or seller for presentation to a Buyer / importer, for either payment on sight (DP) or acceptance of usance Bills of Exchange for payment at a future date (DA). Commitments under collection bills could be post financed in the aforementioned manners" (KFD 2026 p.42).
tenorText: Sight (DP) or Usance (DA, future date)
costText: Import Bills Received for Collection (Trade Tariff 1.13): Sight 2.0% Flat (LKR) / 0.25% Flat (FCY); Acceptance Commission (Usance) 2.0% Flat (LKR) / 0.25% per quarter (FCY); Min LKR 10,000 / USD 40. Acceptance & Payment SWIFT LKR 2,500 / USD 25 each. Unpaid Import Bills at Maturity LKR 4,000 p.m. / USD 40 p.m.
eligibilityText: Importers receiving inward documents from overseas/domestic sellers
collateralText: Goods themselves under DP; case-by-case for DA financing
requiredDocs: Not disclosed; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to T&C importers.

productName: Trade Finance (SME) — Import Loan / Local Purchase Loan
useCaseText: Import Loan. Import Short Term Loans.
description: "Import Loans / Local Purchase Loans: These credit facilities would enable you to clear the goods procured under the L/Cs and retain the same in your custody until sale or conversion and sale in the case of raw materials. The Tenor of such loan facilities range between 30-180 days depending on the nature of the transaction and your working capital cycle. Settlement of the loan would be from the cash flow of the business generated by the sale of the goods imported or procured locally" (KFD 2026 p.42).
tenorText: 30–180 days depending on transaction and working capital cycle
costText: Loan Processing Fee per SME Tariff 1.01–1.02 (0.30%/0.275%, Min LKR 16,500 / USD 66). Acceptances under LC (Trade Tariff): Usance commission applies. Penal 2% p.a. on arrears. Interest rate market-determined.
eligibilityText: SME importers/buyers; typically issued post-LC
valueChainText: Not disclosed (applicable to fabric/inputs importers)
collateralText: Goods imported (Pledge); plus case-by-case additional security
requiredDocs: Not disclosed; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: highly relevant to T&C SMEs sourcing imported fabric, trims, machinery.

productName: Trade Finance (SME) — Export LC Advising / Confirmation
useCaseText: Letter of Credit Advising.
description: Export Letter of Credit Advising and Confirmation services. Confirmation of Sight D/Cs and Usance Documentary Credit issued in favour of the exporter, providing payment certainty.
tenorText: Per underlying LC tenor
costText: Export LC Advising — Cancellation on D/C Advising/Transfer LKR 3,000 / USD 30 (Trade Tariff 2.04). Confirmation of Sight D/Cs (2.05): 0.375% per quarter up to 3 months + 0.125% p.m. additional. Confirmation on Usance D/C (2.06): 1% per quarter for 90 days + 0.125% p.m. beyond; Min LKR 5,000 / USD 100.
guarantee: Confirmation adds Union Bank's payment undertaking.
eligibilityText: Exporters with valid LCs in their favour
valueChainText: Not disclosed (applicable to apparel exporters)
collateralText: Confirmation may require collateral/case-by-case
requiredDocs: Original LC, export documents; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: highly relevant to apparel exporters receiving export LCs.

useCaseText: Packing Credit Loan.
description: "Pre-shipment Credit / Packing Credit facilities: With the durations extending up to 180 days, this could be considered for Exporters who have valid and confirmed export orders from reputed buyers or irrevocable documentary credits issued in their favour, by acceptable banks. Our packing credits enable exporters to effectively finance their export orders with advances up to a pre-agreed % of the value of the export order. Packing credits are also extended to meet the cost of imported raw materials" (KFD 2026 p.43). Recovered from export proceeds or post-shipment bill negotiation.
tenorText: Up to 180 days
costText: Loan Processing Fee per SME Tariff (0.30% New / 0.275% Enhancement). Interest rate at concessional / market export-credit rate (not published). Penal interest 2% p.a. above normal on arrears. CRIB charges LKR 250 / LKR 500.
maxFunding: Pre-agreed % of export order value (KFD 2026)
guarantee: Backed by underlying export order / LC
eligibilityText: Exporters with valid and confirmed export orders from reputed buyers OR irrevocable documentary credits issued in their favour by acceptable banks
valueChainText: Not disclosed (highly applicable to apparel cut-and-sew exporters)
collateralText: Backed by export order or LC; assignment of export proceeds; case-by-case additional
requiredDocs: Export order or LC, business documents; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: KEY product for T&C SME apparel exporters.

useCaseText: Documentary Collection.
description: Export documentary collection service. The bank acts as collecting agent presenting documents for payment (sight) or acceptance (usance) by the overseas buyer.
tenorText: Per underlying bill
costText: Collection — Export Bills - New (Trade Tariff 2.08): 0.25% flat (Foreign and Domestic, Customer); Min LKR 3,500 / USD 35. Non-Customer 0.25% flat; Min LKR 5,000 / USD 50. Free of Payment Export Document Handling fee LKR 3,000 / USD 30 (2.09). Courier Charges Export Bills LKR 6,000 (foreign) / LKR 1,000 (local) / USD 40.
guarantee: None — collection-only basis
eligibilityText: Exporters needing collection-based settlement
collateralText: None for collection-only
requiredDocs: Export documents; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: relevant to T&C exporters using open-account or collection terms.

productName: Trade Finance (SME) — Export Bills Negotiation / Purchase
useCaseText: Bills purchased.
description: "Negotiations of Documentary Bills drawn on D/P or Sight LC terms: This facility enables an Exporter to get immediate cash on his exports without having to await payment from the overseas buyer in respect of the documents… Negotiation of Documentary Bills on D/A Or Usance LC Terms: offers post shipment credit to exporters who ship to buyers on D/A terms by way of purchasing the relevant export bills pending settlement by the Exporter after the supplier credit period" (KFD 2026 p.43).
tenorText: Up to 180 days (per supplier credit period)
costText: Negotiation of Export Bills under D/Cs (Trade Tariff 2.07): 0.25% Flat commission (customer); Min LKR 3,000 / USD 30; Non-customer Min LKR 5,000 / USD 50. Interest at market rate. Loan Processing Fee per SME Tariff applies.
maxFunding: Per bill face value
guarantee: Self-liquidating against export proceeds
eligibilityText: Exporters drawing bills on D/P, Sight LC, or D/A / Usance LC terms
collateralText: Bills themselves; case-by-case for D/A risk
requiredDocs: Export bills, transport documents; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: KEY post-shipment product for apparel exporters.

productName: Trade Finance (SME) — Bonds & Guarantees (Bid Bonds, Performance Bonds, Standby L/Cs, Advance Payment Guarantees)
useCaseText: Bonds & Guarantees. Bid or Tender Bonds. Advance Payment Guarantees. Performance Bonds. Payment Guarantee.
description: "Bid Bonds, Performance Bonds, Standby L/Cs, Letters of Guarantees issued on customers FACILITIES" (Trade Tariff 3.01). Bid/Tender Bonds — "required by principals from prospective parties interested in a particular project or contract as a commitment of their intention to participate in such contract" (KFD 2026 p.43).
tenorText: Per underlying contract
costText: Bid Bonds / Performance Bonds / Standby L/Cs / Letters of Guarantees issued on customers' facilities: 2% p.a. (both LKR and FCY); 1% p.a. against 100% margin accounts; 1.5% p.a. against all interest-bearing deposits/counter-guarantees of banks/standby LCs (Trade Tariff 3.01–3.03). Min Charges (3.04): up to LKR 50,000 = LKR 3,000 / USD 40; above LKR 50,001 = LKR 5,000 / USD 50. Value Enhancements & Extensions as per 3.01/02/03; Amendment charges LKR 2,500 / USD 25. International Guarantees (5.01) issuance against counter-guarantees from Banks 2% p.a.; Min LKR 5,000 / USD 50.
guarantee: Bank guarantee instrument issued on behalf of customer
eligibilityText: Customers participating in tenders, contracts, advance payment arrangements
valueChainText: Not disclosed (relevant to all T&C SMEs participating in buyer tenders)
collateralText: Cash margin (100% for cheapest pricing), interest-bearing deposits, or counter-guarantees; or general credit line
requiredDocs: Underlying contract/tender, application; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to apparel SMEs participating in buyer tenders and contracts.

useCaseText: We offer customised working capital solution for the Small & Medium Enterprises. When you select Union Bank Factoring, you can focus on running your business while we manage your receivables.
description: "Factoring — Financial transaction whereby a business sells its accounts receivables (i.e. Invoices, GRNs) to a third party (called a factor) at a discount in exchange for immediate money with which to finance continued business" — Product Types: Recourse Factoring (KFD 2026 p.52). "Up to 85% advance on your outstanding invoices… Attractive rate of interest" (Factoring product page).
tenorText: Mainly to factor short term instruments — Maximum 120 days for Invoices
costText: "Discount rate, factoring fee and prepayment percentage will be determined by a combination of the following: Monthly sales volume, your customer's credit worthiness and geographical disbursement, invoice sizes, and average payment cycle." (Factoring page). KFD 2026: "Admin. & service fee of 0.1%-0.5% of the face value of instruments discounted (Recovered at the time of discounting). Cheque Hold/Postponement fee of Rs.750/= per cheque". Refinance Schemes page: Factoring rate "To be agreed".
gracePeriod: Not applicable (revolving)
maxFunding: Up to 85% advance on outstanding invoices
guarantee: With recourse — credit risk retained by client
eligibilityText: SMEs with credit-worthy debtors. Submission of a simple application along with an age analysis of your receivables.
valueChainText: Not disclosed (Island-wide branch network)
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis (KFD 2026)
requiredDocs: Simple application form + age analysis of receivables (Factoring page)
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: relevant to T&C SMEs with confirmed buyer invoices.

useCaseText: Post-Dated Cheque (PDC) discounting under Factoring product family.
description: Product Type under Factoring: "Recourse Cheque Discounting" — financing against post-dated cheques received from buyers (KFD 2026 p.52).
tenorText: Maximum 120 days for PD cheques (KFD 2026)
costText: Admin. & service fee 0.1%-0.5% of face value (KFD 2026). Return cheque fee Rs.200/= per cheque; Cheque Hold/Postponement fee Rs.750/= per cheque. Discount rate to be agreed.
guarantee: With recourse
eligibilityText: SMEs receiving post-dated cheques from credit-worthy buyers
collateralText: To be decided on a case-by-case basis
requiredDocs: PDCs, application, age analysis; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: relevant to T&C SMEs receiving PDCs from local buyers.

useCaseText: Distributor Financing under Factoring product family.
description: Product Type under Factoring: "Distributor Financing" — supply-chain finance for distributors of an anchor corporate (KFD 2026 p.52). Working capital financing of distributors against orders/inventory from an anchor manufacturer.
tenorText: Short-term — aligned with distributor cycle
costText: Admin. & service fee 0.1%-0.5% of face value of instruments discounted (KFD 2026 p.52). Discount rate to be agreed; processing fee per SME Tariff.
guarantee: Anchor-supported supply chain arrangement
eligibilityText: Distributors of anchor corporates under a structured programme
valueChainText: Not disclosed (potentially applies to T&C raw-material/yarn distributors)
collateralText: Case-by-case; anchor arrangement
requiredDocs: Anchor agreement, distributor records; contact RM
contactPerson: Customer Service Hotline / SME Assets Center / Relationship Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to T&C distributor/wholesaler segment with anchor manufacturer.

useCaseText: Union Bank's objective is to support and assist the promotion, establishment, expansion and modernisation of industrial, agricultural and commercial ventures in order to encourage and promote participation of private capital, both domestic and foreign in such enterprises. We provide financing for start up projects, existing projects, expansions, diversification etc.
description: "Project financing is a specialised skill. It requires a blend of industry knowledge and financial expertise in order to evaluate the undertaking and structure the optimal funding package for the enterprise. As the project's cash flows are a primary consideration, the evaluation process will include a validation of the project's feasibility… The optimal means of finance would then be determined including the equity contribution and gearing level, the loan tenure and repayment plan, and the credit enhancements that may be required." Sectors financed include Apparels, Manufacturing, Food & Beverage, Hotels & Tourism, Infrastructure, Property Development, Agriculture.
tenorText: Long-term — structured per project (loan tenure determined as part of structuring)
costText: Loan Processing Fee per SME Tariff 1.01 (0.30%, Min LKR 16,500 / USD 66); Penal interest 2% p.a. above normal; Pre-mature settlement 3%/5%. Legal fees for mortgage 0.30%-1.50% of facility per SME Tariff 3.01. Interest market-determined (not published).
gracePeriod: Structured per project (e.g. construction period)
guarantee: None (case-by-case)
eligibilityText: Industrial, agricultural and commercial ventures requiring project funding for promotion, establishment, expansion, modernisation; private capital domestic and foreign.
valueChainText: "Apparels" — explicitly listed as a pioneered sector
collateralText: "Credit enhancements that may be required" — case-by-case
capitalRequirement: Equity contribution and gearing level determined as part of structuring
requiredDocs: Project feasibility study, sponsor track record, management & HR plan, market/technology/production analysis, regulatory approvals (per evaluation process description); contact 0115 800 800
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: "Apparels" explicitly listed as a sector financed by Project Financing. KEY product for T&C SME expansions and modernisations.

productName: SME Transaction Banking — Union Bank BizDirect
useCaseText: Union Bank Biz Direct, is a transaction banking solution that puts control of your business at your fingertips! Designed for corporates and SMEs for easy handling of business banking it provides real-time information and seamless Banking with global access and cross boarder trading.
description: "Domestic & cross-border transfers. Efficient & comprehensive cash flow management. Detailed collection and receivable reporting. Foreign exchange solutions. Transaction alerts. Host2Host payments. Multiple statement options (XML, EXCEL, MT940, PDF). Document attaching option, Invoice based alerts, Customisable trilingual printed instruments… Real-time reporting with information at your fingertips – 24x7x365."
tenorText: On-demand subscription
costText: Per BizDirect Tariff w.e.f. 01.06.2025 (separate published document). Charges include per-transaction fees and Internet Banking Charges. (sources checked: BizDirect Tariff PDF; KFD 2026 p.57)
eligibilityText: Corporates and SMEs
requiredDocs: User Access Request BizDirect+ / BizDirect Lite forms (Application Forms page); Board Resolution for LLCs
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: relevant to T&C SMEs needing payments, FX and cash management.

productName: SME Bancassurance (in partnership with Union Assurance PLC)
useCaseText: Fulfill all your insurance requirements at any Union Bank branch.
description: "Insurance products are offered by our strategic Insurance Partner Union Assurance PLC at attractive rates for Life and General Insurance purposes."

costText: Premiums set by Union Assurance PLC at "attractive rates" — actual pricing not disclosed on Union Bank's site
guarantee: Insurance underwriting by Union Assurance PLC
eligibilityText: SME businesses with insurance needs
requiredDocs: Per Union Assurance underwriting requirements; call 0115 800 800
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: relevant for T&C SMEs needing factory/inventory/transit/business interruption insurance.

useCaseText: Be it business or personal, Union Bank's Leasing provides the ideal leasing solution for you, because we understand your needs and your aspirations and have customised our solutions for you to reach even greater heights.
description: "Customized leasing solutions for personal or business purposes. Registered/ Unregistered (Brand New & Recondition) Vehicles. Low Monthly Rentals. Fast approval. Pay up to 7 years. Up to 70% of the value of the vehicle. Facilities to purchase machinery or equipment. Re finance for existing vehicles. Pre-approved credit card without joining fee."
tenorText: Pay up to 7 years
costText: Leasing Documentation Handling Charges LKR 5,500 + VAT (SME Tariff 7.00). Penal 2% p.a. above normal on arrears. Interest rates available at the Loan Calculator on the site; rate not numerically disclosed on the product page.
maxFunding: Up to 70% of the value of the vehicle
guarantee: Asset itself is leased — title with bank
eligibilityText: Age limit 18-55 years. Salaried and self-employed. A citizen of Sri Lanka holding a valid National Identity Card.
eligibleApplicants: Salaried and self-employed Sri Lankan citizens 18-55

collateralText: Leased asset is the security

processingTime: Not disclosed ("Fast approval")
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: applicable to T&C SMEs purchasing sewing machines, cutting machines, factory equipment, delivery vehicles.

Can be obtained for working capital purposes. No early settlement Fee. Pay up to 30 Months. Registered/ Unregistered (Brand New & Recondition) Vehicles. Fast approval. Free Credit Card. Special insurance premiums."

"Term Loans are granted to accommodate the clients' needs to purchase fixed assets / capital expenditure or to finance permanent working capital requirements with tailor-made repayment periods that match the client's cashflows" (KFD 2026 p.45).
tenorText: Tailor-made to match the client's cashflows

useCaseText: Forward exchange contract
description: "A contractual binding obligation to buy or sell a sum certain in foreign currency at a pre agreed rate of exchange, on or before a future date. The forward price for a currency can be identical with the spot price. However, the forward price quoted is either higher (premium) or lower (discount) than the spot price… the purpose of doing a Forward Exchange Contract can be for commercial reasons, to avoid risk exposure, covering a mismatch of cash, Arbitrage and speculative." (KFD 2026 p.59)
tenorText: On or before a future date (typically up to 1 year)
costText: Forward points (premium/discount). Forward Confirmation/Financing Arrangement Fee USD 50 (Trade Tariff 1.02 — when linked with import LC).
guarantee: Bank counterparty risk; client credit line required
eligibilityText: Companies with underlying trade exposure (importers/exporters)
collateralText: Credit line / margin
requiredDocs: Underlying commercial documents; CBSL forward eligibility tests; contact Treasury
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: KEY hedging tool for T&C exporters receiving USD with LKR cost base.

useCaseText: Repo & Reverse Repo
description: "A contract between the Bank and the customer where the Bank borrows money from the customer against a Government security such as Treasury Bills or Bonds as collateral. The rate of the Repurchase Agreement is determined by the prevailing market interest rates." (KFD 2026 p.59)
tenorText: Short-term money-market
costText: "Determined by prevailing market interest rates" (KFD 2026)
guarantee: Government securities held as collateral
eligibilityText: Customers with surplus funds
eligibleApplicants: Corporates / High-net-worth / SMEs with surplus liquidity

requiredDocs: Per Treasury onboarding
processingTime: Same-day

useCaseText: Treasury Bills
description: "Treasury Bills are short term debt instruments issued by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka on behalf of the Government of Sri Lanka with a fixed interest rate for periods of 3, 6 and 12 months maturity periods. Treasury Bills can also be traded in the secondary market at any given time for capital gains / losses. A sale in Treasury Bills can be carried out on a discounted basis and interest paid is at maturity." (KFD 2026 p.59)
tenorText: 3, 6 and 12 months
costText: Auction-determined discount rate; secondary-market bid/ask spread.
guarantee: Government of Sri Lanka
eligibilityText: Investors with surplus funds
requiredDocs: Per Treasury onboarding
processingTime: Auction-day or secondary market same-day
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: investment product accessible to T&C SMEs with surplus liquidity.

useCaseText: Treasury Bonds
description: "Treasury Bonds are long term debt instruments issued by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka on behalf of the Government of Sri Lanka with a fixed bi-annual coupon interest payment for maturity periods 2 to 30 years. Treasury Bonds are sold at a discount or premium based on the yield to maturity rate and also can be traded in the secondary market at any given time for capital gains / losses. On maturity of a Treasury Bond, the face value of the investment plus coupon interest will be paid." (KFD 2026 p.59)
tenorText: 2 to 30 years
costText: Auction-determined yield; secondary-market bid/ask. Bi-annual coupon.
guarantee: Government of Sri Lanka
eligibilityText: Investors with longer-term surplus funds
requiredDocs: Per Treasury onboarding
processingTime: Auction-day or secondary market
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: long-term investment product for T&C SMEs with surplus liquidity.

productName: Corporate Trade Finance (umbrella — covers Import LCs, Shipping Guarantees, Inward Bills, Import Loans, Pledge Loans, Bid/Tender Bonds, Advance Payment Guarantees, Performance Bonds, Payment Guarantees, LC Advising, Packing Credit Loans, Documentary Collections, Export Bills Purchases)

Their combined strengths cover all you need to know about Imports, Exports, Bonds and other related trade services to provide you the professional guidance and advice that will help you find the most appropriate solutions." Sub-facilities listed identical to SME Trade Finance umbrella (see Rows 4–12 for sub-facility detail). Pricing uniformly per Trade Tariff.

gracePeriod: Not applicable for OD; per facility for STLs
eligibilityText: Corporate clients
collateralText: Case-by-case
requiredDocs: Not disclosed; contact RM

useCaseText: Union Bank Power H.E.R is focused on uplifting women entrepreneurs by providing a free cash management solution along with exclusive benefits to build sustainable businesses.
description: "UB BizDirect cash management solution to enhance business efficiency. Customised funding and flexible repayment plans. Loans for purchasing assets, technology and business expansion (up to 50 Mn). Repayment of up to 5 years (Grace period for Union Bank funded loans and refinance schemes). Access to Grants up to 10% for selected industries (Agriculture, Manufacturing, Tourism & Services). Processing fee waivers. Savings and Investments — Special interest rates (an additional 0.5% p.a. above the normal rate for Savings and FDs and an additional 0.25% for personal FDs of business owners). Special premiums for insurance (Life, Business, Home, Motor and Travel). Business links. Online banking Services, Cheque Books, Advisory Services (Sustainability, ESG best practices, Climate Financing…etc), Credit Card (joining fee waived off), Training and workshops and enrolment to the National Entrepreneurship Development Programme."
tenorText: Repayment of up to 5 years (Grace period for Union Bank funded loans and refinance schemes)
costText: Processing fee waivers; special savings/FD rates (+0.5% / +0.25%); credit card joining fee waived. Loan interest rate not numerically disclosed.

maxFunding: LKR 50 Mn (loans for purchasing assets, technology and business expansion)
guarantee: USAID CATALYZE SL partnership (Jan 2024 agreement supporting women-focused financial and non-financial services)
eligibilityText: Should be a business that is 51% Women owned. OR the following 3 criteria to be met: Founded or led by Woman/Women (30% board representation); 20% shareholding by Women; Key management positions such as CEO/COO to be held by women.

collateralText: Customized — case-by-case

productName: Small & Medium Enterprises Line of Credit 2 (SMELoC 2 — Revolving) [ADB-funded refinance]

Tenor: For investment and joint loans Maximum up to 10 years, with a grace period of up to a maximum of 2 years, if necessary. For non-collateralized working capital loans Maximum up to two (02) years and a grace period is not permitted. Repayment Method: In equal monthly capital instalment together with interest." (KFD 2026 p.52). Funded by Asian Development Bank (ADB) — refinance line secured in 1Q 2022.

Eligible Projects: Micro, Small & Medium Scale projects excluding Trading, Leasing & Renting Businesses / Sectors. Eligible Geographical Areas: All island (SMEs which are in Colombo District are eligible only if they are women-led or have not previously borrowed any business loans).
eligibleApplicants: Micro, Small & Medium enterprises (excluding Trading, Leasing & Renting businesses)

productName: Kapruka Ayojana Loan Scheme [Coconut Cultivation Board refinance]
useCaseText: This is a refinance credit scheme implemented by the Coconut Cultivation Board to develop the coconut industry. Assists farmers by providing loans with attractive fixed interest rate.
description: "Kapruka Ayojana Loan Scheme — Maximum Loan Amount: Rs.10.0Mn. Eligible Projects: New planting, replanting, & rehabilitation of Coconut; Inter-cropping; Coconut seeding nurseries; Irrigation facilities for coconut lands; Small scale dairy farming, goat farming & sheep farming; Micro industries related to the coconut industry; Financing of farm machinery. On Lending Rate: 8% p.a. Tenor for Cultivation: Maximum of 5-year period, with a grace period of 01 year. Nurseries: One year inclusive of 6 months grace period. Equipment finance: 5 years with a grace period of 3 months, if necessary. Borrowers are entitled to reimburse a major portion of the interest cost (i.e. 100% during the 1st year & 50% from 02nd year onwards) from the Coconut Cultivation Board (CCB)." (KFD 2026 p.54)
tenorText: Cultivation: max 5 years (1 yr grace); Nurseries: 1 yr (6 mo grace); Equipment: 5 yrs (3 mo grace if necessary)
costText: On lending rate: 8.00% p.a. (Refinance Schemes page + KFD 2026). Borrowers eligible for CCB interest reimbursement: 100% in Year 1 & 50% from Year 2 onwards. Loan Processing Fee per SME Tariff.
gracePeriod: 1 year (cultivation); 6 months (nurseries); 3 months (equipment)
maxFunding: LKR 10 Mn
guarantee: CCB refinance + CCB interest subsidy (100% Y1 / 50% Y2+)

Tenor: Maximum up to 10 years, with a grace period of up to a maximum of 1 years, if necessary (to be decided on case of basis considering the requirements). Maximum of 24 months of grace period from the date of disbursement of the first installment, only be granted to the affected swine farmers who have received approval by following the guidelines and verification procedures issued by the Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock, Lands and Irrigation. Repayment Method: In equal monthly capital instalment together with interest." (KFD 2026 p.55)

productName: CBSL Disaster Relief Scheme (Cyclonic & Flood) — Circular 04/2025
useCaseText: Relief Measures to Assist Individuals and Businesses affected by the Recent Cyclonic and Flood Disasters by Licensed Banks.
description: "CBSL, in view of the adverse effects of the recent cyclonic and flood disasters… issued the Circular No. 04 of 2025 dated 05.12.2025 to all licensed banks to provide the relief measures to the individuals and businesses whose income or business has been directly affected. Temporary Debt Relief: Suspend the repayments of capital and/or interest of existing credit facilities of the borrowers. For any new loan granted for a longer tenor beyond 2 years, the interest rate may be revised by the licensed bank to a rate linked to Average Weighted Prime Lending Rate (AWPR) at the end of 2 years. Suspend charging for cheque returns, stop payments, late payment fees, credit restructuring/modification fees and penal interest on all credit facilities of affected borrowers up to 31.01.2026. New loan applications not to be declined solely based on adverse CRIB records."
tenorText: Temporary debt relief; new loans up to longer tenor — rate linked to AWPR after 2 years

productName: Union Bank — USAID Climate Adaptation Project Partnership (Agriculture & Tourism sectors)
useCaseText: This partnership will enhance the bank's internal capacity to provide financial and non-financial solutions related to climate adaptation for its clients. description: "Union Bank, as a fully-fledged commercial bank that is committed to advancing Sri Lanka's sustainable development, recently entered into a partnership with the United States Agency for International Development (USAID)-supported Climate Adaptation Project in Sri Lanka. The bank aims to build its capability to provide climate change advice, especially on climate finance solutions for the agriculture sector relating to food security and the tourism sector, with the technical support provided by the USAID Climate Adaptation Project." costText: Fixed and variable rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fees; standard early settlement fees; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable). Applicable interest rate per People's Bank website.
gracePeriod: Maximum grace period of 6 months
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Solar KFD)
maxFunding: 75% of total proposed project cost up to maximum of Rs. 500.0Mn
eligibilityText: Business entity/individual should be registered and operating in Sri Lanka; should be economically and financially viable.
eligibleApplicants: Business entities and individuals operating in Sri Lanka
collateralText: Collateral / security acceptable to the Bank is generally expected
capitalRequirement: 75% project cost financed; balance 25% by applicant (implied)
requiredDocs: Call or visit nearest Branch/service center; subject to financial evaluation; CRIB obligations checked.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: available to business entities/individuals generating solar power (T&C SMEs with solar projects eligible)

useCaseText: The facilities are granted for both investment and working capital requirement of the business which are recognized under the green finance scope.
description: 'Green Power' loans scheme provides financial assistance to environment and social oriented projects, industries, businesses and technologies within the definition of 'Green Finance'. Priority sectors: agriculture, forestry & lodging, manufacturing, electric power generation, gas/steam/AC supply, water supply, sewerage & waste management, construction, tourism & recreation, transportation & storage.
tenorText: Investment loans – maximum 10 years including 2 years' grace period; Working capital Loans – maximum 3 years (no grace period); machinery loans restricted to useful life of equipment
costText: Fixed and variable rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fees; standard early settlement fees; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable). Applicable interest rate per People's Bank website.
gracePeriod: Investment up to 2 years grace; working capital none
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Green Power KFD)
maxFunding: 75% of total proposed project cost up to maximum of Rs. 500.0Mn
eligibilityText: The proposed project should fall within the green finance scope; business entity registered and operating in Sri Lanka; satisfactorily conducted current account for 6 months.
eligibleApplicants: Business entities operating in Sri Lanka in green-finance sectors
businessSizeText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Green Power KFD)
collateralText: Collateral / security acceptable to the Bank is generally expected
capitalRequirement: 75% project cost financed
greenCertRequirement: Project must fall within green finance scope
requiredDocs: Call or visit nearest Branch/service center; subject to financial evaluation; CRIB obligations checked.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: green finance for environment/social oriented businesses incl. manufacturing & water/waste (T&C RECP upgrades eligible)

useCaseText: Term loans are granted for the purpose of Working Capital requirements and capital expenditure activities such as Purchase of commercial Lands/buildings, Construction of commercial buildings, Property Development, Investments, Contracts, Commencement of new business and Other commercial activities.
description: Commercial term loans for business customers, funding working capital and capital expenditure (commercial land/buildings, construction, property development, investments, contracts, new business).
tenorText: Short Term Loans up to 1 year; Medium Term above 1 up to 5 years; Long Term above 5 years
costText: Fixed Rates: 15.0% p.a. (on property mortgage), 16.0% p.a. (on other securities). Floating: Weekly AWPLR + 3.5% p.a. (monthly review, floor rate 12.5% p.a.). 2% additional interest on arrears. Business Loans range 14.0%–17.0% (loan rates table).
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: Term Loan KFD)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Term Loan KFD)
maxFunding: On the need basis upon evaluation
guarantee: Guarantees acceptable to the bank
eligibilityText: Satisfactorily conducted current account for 6 months with our Bank or any other Bank; mainly subject to the repayment capacity of the customer; should have a Business Registration.
businessSizeText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Commercial Credit page, Term Loan KFD)
collateralText: Guarantees, Mortgage over a movable or immovable property or any other security acceptable to the bank.
requiredDocs: Business Registration; 6-month satisfactorily conducted current account; demonstrate repayment capacity.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: general commercial term loan for registered businesses (T&C SMEs eligible)

useCaseText: Our Overdraft facility can be used to finance working capital requirements of your business and to meet any urgent financial needs.
description: Permanent overdraft facility to finance working capital requirements of the business and meet urgent financial needs; interest calculated on usage.
tenorText: 1 Year (facility could be structured for three years subject to annual review/renewal)
costText: Permanent Overdraft 15.50%–18.50% (loan rates table). Interest calculated on usage. Fixed and variable rates; penal interest on overdue amount; standard charges.
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: POD KFD)
maxFunding: On the need basis upon evaluation
eligibilityText: Mainly subjected to the repayment capacity of the customer; should have a Business Registration; current account in the last 6 months.
collateralText: Security acceptable to the bank (general)
requiredDocs: Business Registration; 6-month current account; repayment capacity.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: working capital overdraft for registered businesses (T&C SMEs eligible)

useCaseText: Bank Guarantees are issued to back-up performance/payment obligations on a wide range of contracts. These guarantees constitute an undertaking by the Bank to pay a specific sum of money on demand.
description: Bank Guarantees issued to back up performance and payment obligations on a wide range of contracts; the Bank undertakes to pay a specific sum on demand.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Bank Guarantee KFD)
costText: Local Guarantee issuance commission: Cash-backed (100% margin) 1.5% pro-rata; other acceptable securities (assignment of FD/savings/FC a/c) 2% pro-rata (schedule of charges).
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: Bank Guarantee KFD)
maxFunding: On the need basis upon evaluation
guarantee: The guarantee itself is a risk-sharing/credit-substitute instrument
eligibilityText: Business customers with acceptable securities; subject to financial evaluation.
collateralText: Cash margin or other securities acceptable to the bank (FD/savings/FC a/c assignment)
requiredDocs: Apply at branch; provide acceptable security/margin.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: guarantee facility for business contracts (T&C SMEs eligible)

useCaseText: A Letter of Credit is a written undertaking by a bank on behalf of an importer to pay the exporter against stipulated documents.
description: Import Letters of Credit issued by People's Bank on behalf of importers, undertaking payment to the exporter against compliant documents. Sight and usance L/Cs available, including domestic L/Cs.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Trade Products KFD)
costText: Sight L/C commission: 0.25% of L/C value first quarter + 0.15% per additional month, minimum Rs.5,000. Usance L/C: 0.375% first quarter + 0.15%/month, min Rs.5,000. Amendments min Rs.5,000. (Foreign tariff / schedule of charges.)
eligibilityText: Importers and exporters engaging in international trade; corporate and SME clients; subject to trade-finance documentation and the Bank's evaluation.
businessSizeText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Trade Products KFD)
collateralText: Margin and security acceptable to the bank
requiredDocs: Trade-finance application and underlying trade documents; per Trade Products KFD.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: import LC for traders (T&C SMEs importing fabrics/inputs eligible)

useCaseText: A Shipping Guarantee is issued to enable the importer to take delivery of goods before the arrival of original shipping documents.
description: Shipping Guarantee issued to allow importers to take delivery of goods before original shipping documents arrive.
tenorText: Within 3 months (extendable per additional month)
costText: Commission: within 3 months 0.15% of invoice value, plus per additional month (schedule of charges / Foreign tariff).
eligibilityText: Importers and exporters engaging in international trade; corporate and SME clients; subject to trade-finance documentation and the Bank's evaluation.
collateralText: Margin and security acceptable to the bank
requiredDocs: Trade-finance application; underlying shipping documents.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: shipping guarantee for importers (T&C SMEs importing inputs eligible)

productName: Trade Services — Import Bills / Import Financing
useCaseText: An importer can request for a credit line from the Bank to cover the payment for the Import Bills, payment of Customs Duty, Local Purchases and Working Capital.
description: Import financing covering payment of import bills, customs duty, local purchases and working capital. Types: Short Term Loan – Import (settle bills under LC/collection), Short Term Loan – Local, and Import Trust Receipt.
tenorText: Short-term financing aligned to the trade cycle
costText: Import Finance – Rupee Facilities: Weekly AWPLR + 2.5% to Weekly AWPLR + 4.5% (loan rates table). Plus applicable bill handling/commission per schedule of charges.
eligibilityText: Importers and exporters engaging in international trade; corporate and SME clients; subject to trade-finance documentation and the Bank's evaluation.
collateralText: Security acceptable to the bank; trust receipt
requiredDocs: Trade-finance application; import bills/documents; per Trade Products KFD.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: import financing for traders (T&C SMEs importing fabrics/inputs eligible)

productName: Trade Services — Export Financing (Pre & Post-Shipment / Packing Credit)
useCaseText: Exporters may obtain Pre-Shipment lines of credit at competitive rates to finance their marketing cycles supported by export orders; Post-Shipment financing is also available.
description: Export financing including Pre-Shipment Financing (packing credit, against export LCs or export orders) and Post-Shipment Financing. Short Term Loan – Export available in LKR and foreign currency.
tenorText: Short-term, aligned to the export marketing/shipment cycle
costText: Export Finance – Rupee Facilities: 12.0% to Weekly AWPLR + 4.5% (loan rates table). Plus applicable commission per schedule of charges.
maxFunding: A percentage of the expected export sale proceeds
eligibilityText: Importers and exporters engaging in international trade; corporate and SME clients; subject to trade-finance documentation and the Bank's evaluation. Supported by export orders / export LCs.
collateralText: Export orders/LCs and security acceptable to the bank
requiredDocs: Trade-finance application; export orders/LCs; per Trade Products KFD.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: export financing/packing credit (T&C apparel exporters directly eligible)

useCaseText: Bank Guarantees constitute an undertaking by the Bank to pay specific sum of money on demand to back performance/payment obligations.
description: Bank Guarantees (local and international) issued under the Trade Services suite to back performance and payment obligations on contracts.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Trade Products KFD)
costText: Local Guarantees: Cash-backed 1.5% pro-rata; other acceptable securities 2% pro-rata (schedule of charges – DBU rates).
guarantee: Guarantee is a credit-substitute/risk-sharing instrument
eligibilityText: Importers and exporters engaging in international trade; corporate and SME clients; subject to trade-finance documentation and the Bank's evaluation.
collateralText: Cash margin or securities acceptable to the bank
requiredDocs: Trade-finance application; acceptable security/margin.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: trade guarantees (T&C SMEs eligible)

productName: Trade Services — Domestic Invoice Discounting Facility
useCaseText: Providing advances against bills of quantity / receivables from Government Departments and Corporations for construction work / services.
description: Domestic Invoice Discounting Facility for contractors/service providers: advances against bills of quantity / receivables from Government Departments and Corporations for construction work or services.
tenorText: Short-term, aligned to receivables settlement
costText: Not disclosed (sources checked: Trade Products KFD, schedule of charges) — interest/discount per facility terms.
maxFunding: Determined by receivables value
guarantee: Advance is against assigned receivables
eligibilityText: Contractors / service providers with bills of quantity or receivables from Government Departments and Corporations.
collateralText: Assignment of receivables / bills of quantity
requiredDocs: Assigned receivables / bills of quantity from Government Departments/Corporations.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: receivables discounting for contractors/service providers (T&C SMEs supplying govt entities eligible)

useCaseText: Credit facilities required for cultivation of agricultural crops are granted... to uplift the socio-economic conditions of micro and small scale farmers by funding working capital requirements at lower costs.
description: NCRCS funds working capital for micro and small scale farmers cultivating seasonal (short-term) crops at low cost, under patronage of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka and Ministry of Finance.
tenorText: For Sugarcane and Turmeric – 365 days (max); For other crops – 270 days
costText: Interest Rate 6%. 2% penal interest on overdue installments. Fixed/variable interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fee; standard early settlement charges; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable).
maxFunding: Maximum loan amount Rs. 3.0Mn (Potato cultivation); varies by crop per Ministry of Finance
guarantee: Inter-se Guarantee of two farmers or other acceptable guarantees
eligibilityText: Farmers cultivating paddy, chilies, potatoes, onion, vegetables, grams, soya beans, root & tuber, maize, ginger, oil seeds, sugar cane and other subsidiary crops.
collateralText: Inter-se Guarantee of two farmers or other acceptable guarantees
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch; crop cultivation details.
smeApplicabilityText: RESTRICTED-SECTOR: agricultural crop cultivation scheme; not applicable to T&C apparel SMEs

useCaseText: This loan scheme has been introduced in order to make banking access to the segment of women entrepreneurs engaged in commercially and technically viable and financially feasible business activities.
description: Women-entrepreneur loan scheme to uplift living conditions through emerging women entrepreneurs, for commercially and technically viable, financially feasible business activities.
tenorText: Investment Loans – maximum 5 years including a maximum grace period (if required) of 6 months
costText: Fixed/variable interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fee; standard early settlement charges; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable).
gracePeriod: Maximum 6 months grace (if required)
maxFunding: Rs. 25.0Mn (depends on customer requirement, contribution, purpose, repayment ability, credit worthiness)
guarantee: Convenient securities
eligibilityText: Applicants with an annual turnover less than Rs. 1,000.0Mn are eligible. Age between 25 and 65 years; permanent resident or business within the operational area of the Branch; not a defaulter.
collateralText: Collateral/Security acceptable to the Bank; Business Registration for loans exceeding Rs.1.0Mn
turnoverRequirement: Annual turnover < Rs. 1,000.0Mn
capitalRequirement: 25% of total project cost contributed by applicant from non-borrowing sources
requiredDocs: Visit nearest Branch with last 6 months sales/purchases details or project proposal; CRIB obligations checked.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: women entrepreneurs in any viable business (women-led T&C SMEs eligible)

useCaseText: Aswenna Loan Scheme is a specific agriculture loan scheme for farmers to purchase of paddy Lands.
description: A specific agriculture loan scheme for farmers to purchase paddy lands.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: development & microfinance pages, loan rates table)
costText: Agriculture/Animal Husbandry/Fisheries 14.0%–15.0% (loan rates table). 2% penal interest on overdue.
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page)
eligibilityText: Farmers purchasing paddy lands.
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch.
smeApplicabilityText: RESTRICTED-SECTOR: agriculture/paddy-land scheme; not applicable to T&C apparel SMEs

useCaseText: This loan scheme has been introduced in order to fund investment and working capital requirement of MSMEs to improve their products, services, and related processes at a reasonable rate of interest whilst upgrading their business levels.
description: MSME loan scheme to uplift low-income earners; funds investment and working capital requirements of MSMEs to improve products, services and processes and upgrade business levels.
tenorText: Investment Loans – maximum 5 years including maximum grace period (if required) of 6 months
costText: Fixed/variable interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fee; standard early settlement charges; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable).
gracePeriod: Maximum 6 months grace (if required)
maxFunding: Rs. 5.0Mn (depends on requirement, contribution, purpose, repayment ability, credit worthiness)
guarantee: Convenient securities
eligibilityText: Facilities for any commercially viable and profitable business operating in Sri Lanka. Applicants with annual turnover up to Rs. 25.0Mn. Age below 65 years; not a defaulter.
collateralText: Convenient securities; Business Registration for loans exceeding Rs.1.0Mn
turnoverRequirement: Annual turnover up to Rs. 25.0Mn
requiredDocs: Visit nearest branch; business details; CRIB checked.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: MSME loan for any viable business (micro/small T&C SMEs eligible)

useCaseText: Kapruka Ayojana loan scheme... in collaboration with the Coconut Cultivation Board (CCB), to provide investment capital for the development of coconut lands.
description: Concessionary financial assistance (with the Coconut Cultivation Board) providing investment capital for development of coconut lands as whole farm units under 10 development categories.
tenorText: Maximum 5 Years (including maximum 12 months grace period)
costText: Interest Rate 8% per annum. 2% penal interest on overdue installments. Fixed/variable interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fee; standard early settlement charges; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable).
gracePeriod: Maximum 12 months grace
maxFunding: Rs. 5.0Mn per borrower (refinance page states Rs. 10.0Mn per borrower)
guarantee: Two personal guarantors acceptable to the Bank and mortgage over a movable or immovable property
eligibilityText: Coconut growers; for coconut new planting/replanting/inter-cropping, rehabilitation, drip/hose irrigation, farm machinery, nursery establishment, crop protection, cattle/sheep/goat farming under coconut.
collateralText: Two personal guarantors and mortgage over movable/immovable property
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch; coconut land development details.
smeApplicabilityText: RESTRICTED-SECTOR: coconut cultivation development scheme; not applicable to T&C apparel SMEs

useCaseText: Small Holder Agribusiness Partnership Programme is a special loan scheme introduced... [in accordance with the programme].
description: A special agribusiness loan scheme (Small Holder Agribusiness Partnership Programme) supporting smallholder agribusiness development.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: development loans page, loan rates table)
costText: Development Loans 6.5%–20.0%; Agriculture-related 14.0%–15.0% (loan rates table). 2% penal interest on overdue.
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page)
eligibilityText: Smallholder agribusiness participants under the programme.
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch.
smeApplicabilityText: RESTRICTED-SECTOR: smallholder agribusiness programme; not applicable to T&C apparel SMEs

useCaseText: This loan scheme has been introduced to finance working capital requirement of potential mill owners and direct buyers... to ensure reasonable price for paddy and maize harvest of farmers during the harvesting period.
description: Pledge loan scheme providing financial support to rice millers and wholesale buyers for purchase and storage of paddy and maize, financing working capital during the harvesting period.
tenorText: For Paddy – 180 Days; For Maize – 270 Days
costText: Fixed interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital. Applicable interest rate per People's Bank website.
maxFunding: No maximum loan limit — determined based on store capacity, grain yield, market trends and financial feasibility
guarantee: Pledge of stored paddy/maize stock
eligibilityText: Potential mill owners and direct buyers of paddy and maize with adequate storage capacity; in business not less than 12 months.
collateralText: Pledge over stored paddy/maize; security acceptable to the bank
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch; storage and business details.
smeApplicabilityText: RESTRICTED-SECTOR: paddy/maize millers & buyers scheme; not applicable to T&C apparel SMEs

productName: SEPI-II — Self-Employment Promotion Initiative Loan Scheme (Phase II)
useCaseText: Provide financial assistance to young entrepreneurs for establishment of self-employment projects.
description: Loan scheme providing financial assistance to trained youth who passed out from recognised vocational training institutions for establishment of their own self-employment projects.
tenorText: Maximum repayment period 5 years, including a maximum grace period (if required) of 6 months
costText: Interest Rate 7.0% p.a. Fixed/variable interest rates; penal interest on overdue capital & interest; standard loan processing fee; standard early settlement charges; valuation/legal charges, govt stamp duty, insurance premium (if applicable).
gracePeriod: Maximum 6 months grace (if required)
maxFunding: Maximum Rs. 1.0Mn (depends on requirement, contribution, purpose, repayment ability, credit worthiness)
eligibilityText: Trained youth who passed out from recognized vocational training institutions in Sri Lanka, for establishing self-employment projects.
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch with vocational training qualification and project proposal.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: youth self-employment scheme; T&C self-employment projects by vocationally-trained youth eligible

useCaseText: People's Bank is ready to support your recovery with a special concessionary loan facility at an interest rate of just 3%.
description: A special concessionary disaster-relief loan facility at 3% interest for businesses affected by the recent natural disaster.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page)
costText: Interest rate of just 3% (concessionary).
maxFunding: Up to Rs. 1,000,000 for SME Businesses; Up to Rs. 250,000 for Micro Enterprises
eligibilityText: Businesses affected by the recent natural disaster; SME businesses and micro enterprises.
businessSizeText: SME Businesses up to Rs. 1,000,000; Micro Enterprises up to Rs. 250,000
collateralText: Not disclosed (contact Branch Manager)
requiredDocs: Contact the Branch Manager at nearest People's Bank branch or listed district officials.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: disaster-affected SMEs/micro enterprises (T&C SMEs affected by disaster eligible)

useCaseText: Development loans with the lowest interest rates. Tailored for businesses and entrepreneurs.
description: Development loans tailored for businesses and entrepreneurs (umbrella development-lending category covering agricultural, agribusiness and rural development schemes).
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, loan rates table)
costText: Development Loans 6.5%–20.0% (loan rates table). 2% penal interest on overdue.
maxFunding: For Working Capital Purposes Rs. 2.0Mn (per Google snippet of development-loans page)
eligibilityText: Businesses and entrepreneurs engaged in development/priority sectors.
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: development lending for businesses/entrepreneurs (T&C SMEs in priority sectors eligible)

useCaseText: Vehicles can be purchased to uplift your lifestyle (personal use), or for your business success with 'People's Auto Loan' with a security acceptable to the bank.
description: Vehicle financing for personal or business use; vehicles can be purchased for business success with security acceptable to the bank.
tenorText: Up to 8 years
costText: Interest rate: 13.5%–15.0% per annum. Vehicle Loans 12.5%–14.0% (loan rates table). 2% penal interest on overdue installments.
maxFunding: No Upper Limit
guarantee: Mortgage over the vehicle to be purchased
eligibilityText: Individuals and businesses purchasing a vehicle with security acceptable to the bank.
collateralText: Mortgage over the vehicle to be purchased
requiredDocs: Apply at nearest branch; vehicle and income details.
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: vehicle finance for business use (T&C SMEs financing business vehicles eligible)

useCaseText: Customized financial solutions tailored to the specific needs of potentially viable and feasible businesses... such as debt restructuring, rescheduling, or other financial arrangements.
description: The Business Revival Unit assists viable businesses in financial distress, offering customized solutions such as debt restructuring, rescheduling or other financial arrangements (per CBSL Circular No. 02 of 2024).
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page)
costText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page) — terms framed per the Bank's credit policy and CBSL regulatory frameworks.
guarantee: Revival plans framed under the Bank's internal credit policy and CBSL frameworks
eligibilityText: Businesses that maintain credit facilities with People's Bank, are experiencing operational/financial challenges, demonstrate recovery potential, and commit to transparent collaboration.
eligibleApplicants: Existing People's Bank business borrowers in distress
collateralText: Per existing facility / restructuring arrangement
requiredDocs: Referral by People's Bank branches; viability/feasibility assessment.
contactPerson: Business Revival Unit AGM / Chief Manager
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: distressed-business revival for existing PB borrowers (distressed T&C SMEs eligible)

maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, tariff)

productName: Working Capital Finance (SME) — Short Term Loan
useCaseText: Short term facilities are available to finance short-term working capital requirements
description: Short-term facilities to finance short-term working capital requirements, structured in line with the working capital cycle; flexible terms and quick access to funds.
tenorText: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page (short-term by nature))

useCaseText: 1. The Micro Mortgage product offers affordable financing for individuals seeking to buy or renovate small properties. With lower loan amounts and flexible repayment options, it caters to first-time homeowners and those with limited access to traditional mortgages. 2. The purpose of the Micro Mortgage is to facilitate you to purchase of bare land, construction, renovation, extension & repairs of house, purchase of a fully completed house or condominium for residential/commercial purpose, working capital requirements and to fund the business startups.
description: Money can solve all your financial problems. We at Asia Asset invest all our efforts to up your financial lifestyle. This is the fastest way for you to raise funds. Mortgage your asset with us, generate money and in time reclaim your asset. […] The Micro Mortgage product offers affordable financing for individuals seeking to buy or renovate small properties. […] Purpose: purchase of bare land, construction, renovation, extension & repairs of house, purchase of a fully completed house or condominium for residential/commercial purpose, working capital requirements and to fund the business startups.
tenorText: Loan repayment period up to maximum 05 years
costText: Interest Rate 20%-35% per annum (fixed or variable, determined on credit risk of the client). Service Charge 1% (of Capital). CRIB Charge Rs.1200. Penal rate of 4% p.m. applicable only after 7 days of grace period. Other charges: Search Fee, Valuation Fee, Postage, Stamp Duty, Mortgage Fee, Loan Protection Insurance Cover Fee, Processing Charges. Overdue interest at the statutory rate of the Monetary Board of CBSL. (Source: Charges & Tariff Sheet – Mortgage)
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
eligibilityText: Individuals applicant should be a Sri Lankan citizen of age above 18. Loan repayment period up to maximum 05 years. Extents of lands shall be equal to or more than the minimum extents prescribed in Local Government Authority related laws (Municipal Council, Urban Council and Pradeshiya Sabha). Said facilities extended to ages between 25 to 60 Years. Characteristics: (i) Employed or self employed; (ii) Preferably with a family; (iii) No history of a Criminal Record; (iv) CRIB should indicate his respect to honor his obligations; (v) Living in the same premises for over 5 years would an advantage (but not a restrictive condition); (vi) Reference to be obtained by neighbors or work colleagues.
eligibleApplicants: Individuals (Sri Lankan citizens, employed or self-employed); first-time homeowners; those with limited access to traditional mortgages; small business startups
businessSizeText: Micro Mortgage facilities will focus on the Citizens of Sri Lanka with a good track record which has genuine intent to respect the laws of the country and adhere to the rules and regulations.
collateralText: Property Related Documents (Title Deed, Survey Plan etc.) — mortgage over land/house
requiredDocs: 1. Identification proof – NIC / Driving License (DL) / Passport (PP). 2. Address proof – NIC / Utility Bill / Grama Niladari Certificate or any other confirmation from the local authority. 3. Income proof – Salary Slips / Bank Documents / other income proof documents (Affidavit attested by the Grama Niladari). 4. Guarantor details – NIC/DL/PP, Billing proof / Income proof. 5. Other requirements: Duly completed application form; Property Related Documents (Title Deed, Survey Plan etc.).
applicationForm: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: Micro Mortgage facilities will focus on the Citizens of Sri Lanka with a good track record. Includes working capital requirements and funding business startups — accessible to T&C SME owner-operators.

institutionType: Non-Banking Financial Institution (NBFI)  - (Inlcuding leasing companies and factoring companies)
useCaseText: Description: Leasing: Carrying the principles of Commitment to customers as the top priority, here's the leasing solutions offered to you, whether you're a professional individual, businessmen or corporate body. Leasing Product. Purpose: To invest money for the acquisition of vehicles and equipment. -Facilities to purchase cars, lorries, jeeps, Vans, three wheelers, Motor cycles, agricultural, solar system. The customers would get the following benefits under the fixed interest digital leasing product.
description: Don't let anyone tell you that you should not drive your dream vehicle NOW! Any vehicle you could have possibly wanted to drive or ride is just one visit to your nearest Asia Asset Branch away. […] When you apply for a lease from us our trained professionals will provide you with; The finest interest rates in the market, Doorstep Service, Minimum Documentation, Zero Hassle, Flexible Repayment Terms & etc. […] -Facilities to purchase cars, lorries, jeeps, Vans, three wheelers, Motor cycles, agricultural, solar system.
tenorText: Fixed rates applicable for leasing products is decided on the type of facility, asset, tenure, and client's credit worthiness and the security offered.
costText: Interest Rate 15%-28% per annum (varies by facility, asset, tenure, client credit risk, security). Service Charges depend on facility amount. CRIB charge Rs.1500. Stamp Duty 1% from Total Receivable for Private use vehicles (Only B Class Vehicles - Car/Van/Dual purpose). Call charge and Letter charge at actual. Other charges: Processing/Documentation/Stationery, Vehicle Inspection Fees, Postage, Stamp Fees, RMV Fees, Insurance Premiums, Penal rates. Penal interest rate 3% per month (comprehensive Insurance is mandatory) applicable after 5 days grace period. In default: cost of visits/stationery, Repossession chargers, RMV chargers at disposal. (Source: Charges & Tariff Sheet – Leasing)
gracePeriod: 5 days grace period before penal interest is applied
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
guarantee: Comprehensive Insurance mandatory with an Insurer of Lessee's choice (Lessor can introduce an Insurer with Lessee's consent)
eligibilityText: Customers can apply to this product with the following details. Individuals: 1. Identification proof – (NIC/DL/PP); 2. TIN Number; 3. Address proof – (NIC/Utility bill/GS certificate); 4. Income proof – (Salary Slips/Bank Documents/other income proof documents/TIN number); 5. Guarantor details – (ID proof/Billing); 6. Vehicle details – Second Hand - CR / Valuation / Revenue License; Brand new – Invoices; Unregistered – Dealer Invoices / Custom clearance document; 7. Supplier details / Vehicle details. For Corporate customers: 1. Identification proof – (BR/form 41 or form 1 / form 20, Article and resolutions); 2. Address proof – (Form 13); 3. Income proof – (Financial Statements/Bank Documents/other income proof documents/ Audited Accounts / Management Accounts); 4. Guarantor details – (ID proof/Billing proof/Income Proof).
businessSizeText: The product is targeted both individuals and business enterprises with able repayment capacity to offer this product. * Individuals - above 20 years and below 65years * Partnerships * Companies * Farmers /laborers * Entrepreneurs
collateralText: The leased asset itself (vehicle/equipment/solar system) — finance lease under Finance Leasing Act No 56 of 2000
requiredDocs: Individuals: NIC/DL/PP; TIN Number; Address proof (NIC/Utility bill/GS certificate); Income proof (Salary Slips/Bank Documents); Guarantor details (ID proof/Billing); Vehicle details (Second-hand: CR/Valuation/Revenue License; Brand new: Invoices; Unregistered: Dealer Invoices/Custom clearance); Supplier details. Corporate: BR/Form 41 or Form 1/Form 20, Article and resolutions; Form 13 (address); Financial Statements/Bank Documents/Audited Accounts/Management Accounts (income); Guarantor details.
applicationForm: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: Both individuals and business enterprises with able repayment capacity (individuals 20–65, partnerships, companies, farmers/laborers, entrepreneurs) — directly available to T&C SMEs for vehicle, equipment and solar-system financing.

useCaseText: Re Financing / Sale and leased back — facility under the AAF leasing product enabling customers to release cash against an existing, owned asset (typically a vehicle or equipment) by transferring it to AAF and leasing it back. Same eligibility, documentation, security regime and charges as the underlying Leasing product.
description: Re Financing /Sale and leased back. (Sub-row in the Leasing KFD table, applying the same product terms as the Leasing product.)
tenorText: Fixed rates applicable; tenure decided on the type of facility, asset, and client's credit worthiness and the security offered.
costText: Same charging regime as the Leasing product: Interest Rate 15%-28% per annum; Service Charges depend on Facility Amount; CRIB Rs.1500; Stamp Duty 1% on Total Receivable (B-Class Private vehicles); Call/Letter charge at actual; plus Processing/Documentation, Vehicle Inspection, Postage, Stamp, RMV, Insurance Premiums, Penal rates (3% p.m. after 5-day grace). (Source: KFD-Leasing; Charges & Tariff Sheet – Leasing)
gracePeriod: 5 days grace period before penal interest
minFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
maxFunding: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD, tariff sheet)
guarantee: Comprehensive Insurance mandatory
eligibilityText: Same as Leasing product: Individuals above 20 and below 65 years, Partnerships, Companies, Farmers/Laborers, Entrepreneurs with able repayment capacity. Customer must own the asset being sold and leased back; AAF takes title and leases it back.
businessSizeText: Same target as Leasing: individuals (20–65), partnerships, companies, farmers/laborers, entrepreneurs.
collateralText: The asset transferred to AAF and leased back
requiredDocs: Same as Leasing product (Individuals and Corporate documentation lists as per KFD-Leasing): NIC/DL/PP, TIN, Address proof, Income proof, Guarantor details, Vehicle/asset details (CR, valuation, etc.).
applicationForm: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)
smeApplicabilityText: OPEN: Same eligibility as Leasing — available to T&C SMEs that own eligible vehicles/equipment and need to release working capital.

institutionType: Non-Banking Financial Institution (NBFI)  - (Inlcuding leasing companies and factoring companies)
useCaseText: Lease your 4-Wheeler (registered or unregistered vehicles) with flexible repayment plans.
description: Abans Finance offers you the easiest and most convenient way to lease your 4-Wheeler (registered or unregistered vehicles). You can visit any of our Branches or you can call right from your home where one of our Marketing Officers will visit you to grant the Lease.
tenorText: Minimum 1 year & maximum 5 years
costText: Rates as per tariff sheet decided by the Management; rates available upon inquiry. Service Charges: Up to Rs 2Mn – Rs 7,500/- (subject to 5%); Rs 2Mn–Rs 5Mn – Rs 15,000/-; Rs 5Mn–Rs 7.5Mn – Rs 30,000/-; Rs 7.5Mn+ – Rs 40,000/-. LCV: Min Rs 6,000/- Max Rs 10,000/-. Asset transfer fees, government tax/stamp duty applicable. (Source: KFD p.1-2; Lending Rates PDF p.3-4, w.e.f. 01 Aug 2022)
gracePeriod: Not disclosed (sources checked: product page, KFD)

Contact details and National Identity Card number of a personal Guarantor.

Service Charges (w.e.f. 01 Aug 2022): Registered – facility Rs 70k-80k: 5%; Rs 80k-100k: Rs 4,000/-; Rs 100k+: Rs 5,000/-. Unregistered – ≤Rs 100k: 5%; >Rs 100k: Rs 5,000/-. Asset transfer fees, gov tax/stamp duty applicable.
